method.name: version_string
method.description: The SQLite version as a string in the format 'X.Y.Z'.

method.name: version_number
method.description: The SQLite version number as an integer: X*1000000 + Y*1000 + Z.

method.name: source_id
method.description: The unique source identifier for this SQLite build (date, time, and hash).

method.name: scm_branch
method.description: SCM branch name for this SQLite source tree.

method.name: scm_tags
method.description: SCM tags for this SQLite source tree.

method.name: scm_datetime
method.description: SCM datetime for this SQLite source tree.

method.name: libversion
method.description: Returns the SQLite library version as a string.

method.name: sourceid
method.description: Returns the source ID (SHA1 hash) identifying the exact SQLite build.

method.name: libversion_number
method.description: Returns the SQLite library version as an integer (see SQLITE_VERSION_NUMBER macro).

method.name: compileoption_used
method.description: Returns 1 if the specified compile-time option was defined, 0 otherwise. The SQLITE_ prefix may be omitted from the option name.

method.name: compileoption_get
method.description: Returns the N-th compile time option string or an empty string if N is out of range. The SQLITE_ prefix is omitted in the returned string.

method.name: threadsafe
method.description: Returns nonzero if SQLite was compiled with mutexes enabled (threadsafe), or 0 if mutexes were omitted at compile time. This only reflects the compile-time setting, not any runtime changes.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes and destroys the given SQLite database handle. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful; SQLITE_BUSY if there are unfinalized statements or open blobs/backups. Safe to call with null or already closed handles.

method.name: ok
method.description: Successful result.

method.name: error
method.description: Generic error.

method.name: internal
method.description: Internal logic error in SQLite.

method.name: perm
method.description: Access permission denied.

method.name: abort
method.description: Callback routine requested an abort.

method.name: busy
method.description: The database file is locked.

method.name: locked
method.description: A table in the database is locked.

method.name: nomem
method.description: A malloc() failed.

method.name: readonly
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database.

method.name: interrupt
method.description: Operation terminated by sqlite3_interrupt().

method.name: ioerr
method.description: Some kind of disk I/O error occurred.

method.name: corrupt
method.description: The database disk image is malformed.

method.name: notfound
method.description: Unknown opcode in sqlite3_file_control().

method.name: full
method.description: Insertion failed because database is full.

method.name: cantopen
method.description: Unable to open the database file.

method.name: protocol
method.description: Database lock protocol error.

method.name: empty
method.description: Internal use only.

method.name: schema
method.description: The database schema changed.

method.name: toobig
method.description: String or BLOB exceeds size limit.

method.name: constraint
method.description: Abort due to constraint violation.

method.name: mismatch
method.description: Data type mismatch.

method.name: misuse
method.description: Library used incorrectly.

method.name: nolfs
method.description: Uses OS features not supported on host.

method.name: auth
method.description: Authorization denied.

method.name: format
method.description: Not used.

method.name: range
method.description: 2nd parameter to sqlite3_bind out of range.

method.name: notadb
method.description: File opened that is not a database file.

method.name: notice
method.description: Notifications from sqlite3_log().

method.name: warning
method.description: Warnings from sqlite3_log().

method.name: row
method.description: sqlite3_step() has another row ready.

method.name: done
method.description: sqlite3_step() has finished executing.

method.name: error_missing_collseq
method.description: Extended error: Missing collation sequence.

method.name: error_retry
method.description: Extended error: Retry requested.

method.name: error_snapshot
method.description: Extended error: Snapshot error.

method.name: error_reservesize
method.description: Extended error: Reserve size error.

method.name: error_key
method.description: Extended error: Key error.

method.name: error_unable
method.description: Extended error: Unable error.

method.name: ioerr_read
method.description: Disk I/O error: read.

method.name: ioerr_short_read
method.description: Disk I/O error: short read.

method.name: ioerr_write
method.description: Disk I/O error: write.

method.name: ioerr_fsync
method.description: Disk I/O error: fsync.

method.name: ioerr_dir_fsync
method.description: Disk I/O error: directory fsync.

method.name: ioerr_truncate
method.description: Disk I/O error: truncate.

method.name: ioerr_fstat
method.description: Disk I/O error: fstat.

method.name: ioerr_unlock
method.description: Disk I/O error: unlock.

method.name: ioerr_rdlock
method.description: Disk I/O error: read lock.

method.name: ioerr_delete
method.description: Disk I/O error: delete.

method.name: ioerr_blocked
method.description: Disk I/O error: blocked.

method.name: ioerr_nomem
method.description: Disk I/O error: no memory.

method.name: ioerr_access
method.description: Disk I/O error: access.

method.name: ioerr_checkreservedlock
method.description: Disk I/O error: check reserved lock.

method.name: ioerr_lock
method.description: Disk I/O error: lock.

method.name: ioerr_close
method.description: Disk I/O error: close.

method.name: ioerr_dir_close
method.description: Disk I/O error: directory close.

method.name: ioerr_shmopen
method.description: Disk I/O error: shared memory open.

method.name: ioerr_shmsize
method.description: Disk I/O error: shared memory size.

method.name: ioerr_shmlock
method.description: Disk I/O error: shared memory lock.

method.name: ioerr_shmmap
method.description: Disk I/O error: shared memory map.

method.name: ioerr_seek
method.description: Disk I/O error: seek.

method.name: ioerr_delete_noent
method.description: Disk I/O error: delete no entry.

method.name: ioerr_mmap
method.description: Disk I/O error: mmap.

method.name: ioerr_gettemppath
method.description: Disk I/O error: get temporary path.

method.name: ioerr_convpath
method.description: Disk I/O error: convert path.

method.name: ioerr_vnode
method.description: Disk I/O error: vnode.

method.name: ioerr_auth
method.description: Disk I/O error: auth.

method.name: ioerr_begin_atomic
method.description: Disk I/O error: begin atomic.

method.name: ioerr_commit_atomic
method.description: Disk I/O error: commit atomic.

method.name: ioerr_rollback_atomic
method.description: Disk I/O error: rollback atomic.

method.name: ioerr_data
method.description: Disk I/O error: data.

method.name: ioerr_corruptfs
method.description: Disk I/O error: corrupt file system.

method.name: ioerr_in_page
method.description: Disk I/O error: in page.

method.name: ioerr_badkey
method.description: Disk I/O error: bad key.

method.name: ioerr_codec
method.description: Disk I/O error: codec.

method.name: locked_sharedcache
method.description: A table in the database is locked (shared cache).

method.name: locked_vtab
method.description: A table in the database is locked (virtual table).

method.name: busy_recovery
method.description: Database is busy (recovery).

method.name: busy_snapshot
method.description: Database is busy (snapshot).

method.name: busy_timeout
method.description: Database is busy (timeout).

method.name: cantopen_notempdir
method.description: Unable to open the database file (no temp dir).

method.name: cantopen_isdir
method.description: Unable to open the database file (is a directory).

method.name: cantopen_fullpath
method.description: Unable to open the database file (full path).

method.name: cantopen_convpath
method.description: Unable to open the database file (convert path).

method.name: cantopen_dirtywal
method.description: Unable to open the database file (dirty WAL, Not Used).

method.name: cantopen_symlink
method.description: Unable to open the database file (symlink).

method.name: corrupt_vtab
method.description: Database disk image is malformed (virtual table).

method.name: corrupt_sequence
method.description: Database disk image is malformed (sequence).

method.name: corrupt_index
method.description: Database disk image is malformed (index).

method.name: readonly_recovery
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database (recovery).

method.name: readonly_cantlock
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database (can't lock).

method.name: readonly_rollback
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database (rollback).

method.name: readonly_dbmoved
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database (db moved).

method.name: readonly_cantinit
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database (can't init).

method.name: readonly_directory
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database (directory).

method.name: abort_rollback
method.description: Abort due to rollback.

method.name: constraint_check
method.description: Constraint violation: check constraint.

method.name: constraint_commithook
method.description: Constraint violation: commit hook.

method.name: constraint_foreignkey
method.description: Constraint violation: foreign key.

method.name: constraint_function
method.description: Constraint violation: function.

method.name: constraint_notnull
method.description: Constraint violation: NOT NULL.

method.name: constraint_primarykey
method.description: Constraint violation: primary key.

method.name: constraint_trigger
method.description: Constraint violation: trigger.

method.name: constraint_unique
method.description: Constraint violation: unique.

method.name: constraint_vtab
method.description: Constraint violation: virtual table.

method.name: constraint_rowid
method.description: Constraint violation: rowid.

method.name: constraint_pinned
method.description: Constraint violation: pinned.

method.name: constraint_datatype
method.description: Constraint violation: datatype.

method.name: notice_recover_wal
method.description: Notice: recover WAL.

method.name: notice_recover_rollback
method.description: Notice: recover rollback.

method.name: notice_rbu
method.description: Notice: RBU.

method.name: warning_autoindex
method.description: Warning: autoincrement index.

method.name: auth_user
method.description: Authorization denied by user.

method.name: ok_load_permanently
method.description: Successful result: loaded permanently.

method.name: ok_symlink
method.description: Successful result: symlink (internal use only).

method.name: open_readonly
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): open in read-only mode.

method.name: open_readwrite
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): open in read-write mode.

method.name: open_create
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): create if not exists.

method.name: open_deleteonclose
method.description: Bitmask flag: delete on close (VFS only).

method.name: open_exclusive
method.description: Bitmask flag: exclusive access (VFS only).

method.name: open_autoproxy
method.description: Bitmask flag: auto proxy (VFS only).

method.name: open_uri
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): URI filename interpretation.

method.name: open_memory
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): open in-memory database.

method.name: open_main_db
method.description: Bitmask flag: main database file (VFS only).

method.name: open_temp_db
method.description: Bitmask flag: temporary database file (VFS only).

method.name: open_transient_db
method.description: Bitmask flag: transient database file (VFS only).

method.name: open_main_journal
method.description: Bitmask flag: main journal file (VFS only).

method.name: open_temp_journal
method.description: Bitmask flag: temporary journal file (VFS only).

method.name: open_subjournal
method.description: Bitmask flag: subjournal file (VFS only).

method.name: open_super_journal
method.description: Bitmask flag: super journal file (VFS only).

method.name: open_nomutex
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): no mutex.

method.name: open_fullmutex
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): full mutex.

method.name: open_sharedcache
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): shared cache.

method.name: open_privatecache
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): private cache.

method.name: open_wal
method.description: Bitmask flag: write-ahead log (VFS only).

method.name: open_nofollow
method.description: Bitmask flag for sqlite3_open_v2(): do not follow symbolic links.

method.name: open_exrescode
method.description: Bitmask flag: extended result codes.

method.name: open_master_journal
method.description: Bitmask flag: master journal file (VFS only, legacy compatibility).

method.name: iocap_atomic
method.description: Device characteristic: all writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic512
method.description: Device characteristic: 512-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic1k
method.description: Device characteristic: 1K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic2k
method.description: Device characteristic: 2K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic4k
method.description: Device characteristic: 4K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic8k
method.description: Device characteristic: 8K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic16k
method.description: Device characteristic: 16K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic32k
method.description: Device characteristic: 32K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_atomic64k
method.description: Device characteristic: 64K-byte writes are atomic.

method.name: iocap_safe_append
method.description: Device characteristic: safe append.

method.name: iocap_sequential
method.description: Device characteristic: sequential writes.

method.name: iocap_undeletable_when_open
method.description: Device characteristic: file cannot be deleted when open.

method.name: iocap_powersafe_overwrite
method.description: Device characteristic: powersafe overwrite.

method.name: iocap_immutable
method.description: Device characteristic: file is immutable.

method.name: iocap_batch_atomic
method.description: Device characteristic: batch atomic writes.

method.name: iocap_subpage_read
method.description: Device characteristic: subpage read.

method.name: lock_none
method.description: File lock: none (xUnlock() only).

method.name: lock_shared
method.description: File lock: shared.

method.name: lock_reserved
method.description: File lock: reserved.

method.name: lock_pending
method.description: File lock: pending.

method.name: lock_exclusive
method.description: File lock: exclusive.

method.name: sync_normal
method.description: Synchronization: normal fsync() semantics.

method.name: sync_full
method.description: Synchronization: Mac OS X style fullsync.

method.name: sync_dataonly
method.description: Synchronization: flush data only, not inode.

method.name: fcntl_lockstate
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_LOCKSTATE opcode is used for debugging. It causes the xFileControl method to write the current state of the lock into an integer that the pArg argument points to.

method.name: fcntl_get_lockproxyfile
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_GET_LOCKPROXYFILE opcode is used with the xFileControl method to retrieve the current lock proxy file.

method.name: fcntl_set_lockproxyfile
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_SET_LOCKPROXYFILE opcode is used with the xFileControl method to set a new lock proxy file.

method.name: fcntl_last_errno
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_LAST_ERRNO opcode is used to obtain the last operating system error number associated with a file.

method.name: fcntl_size_hint
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_SIZE_HINT opcode gives the VFS layer a hint about how large the database file will grow during the current transaction.

method.name: fcntl_chunk_size
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CHUNK_SIZE opcode requests the VFS to extend and truncate the database file in user-specified chunk sizes.

method.name: fcntl_file_pointer
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_FILE_POINTER opcode is used to obtain a pointer to the sqlite3_file object associated with a particular database connection.

method.name: fcntl_sync_omitted
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_SYNC_OMITTED file-control is no longer used.

method.name: fcntl_win32_av_retry
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_WIN32_AV_RETRY opcode configures automatic retry counts and intervals for certain disk I/O operations for the Windows VFS.

method.name: fcntl_persist_wal
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_PERSIST_WAL opcode is used to set or query the persistent Write Ahead Log (WAL) setting.

method.name: fcntl_overwrite
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_OVERWRITE opcode is invoked after opening a write transaction to indicate that the entire database file will be overwritten by the current transaction.

method.name: fcntl_vfsname
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_VFSNAME opcode can be used to obtain the names of all VFSes in the VFS stack.

method.name: fcntl_powersafe_overwrite
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_POWERSAFE_OVERWRITE opcode is used to set or query the persistent powersafe-overwrite (PSOW) setting.

method.name: fcntl_pragma
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_PRAGMA file control is sent to the open sqlite3_file object corresponding to the database file to which a pragma statement refers.

method.name: fcntl_busyhandler
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_BUSYHANDLER opcode provides a custom VFS with access to the connection's busy-handler callback.

method.name: fcntl_tempfilename
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_TEMPFILENAME file-control generates a temporary filename using SQLite's internal algorithm.

method.name: fcntl_mmap_size
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_MMAP_SIZE file control is used to query or set the maximum number of bytes for memory-mapped I/O.

method.name: fcntl_trace
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_TRACE file control provides advisory information to the VFS about what higher layers of the SQLite stack are doing.

method.name: fcntl_has_moved
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_HAS_MOVED file control writes a boolean indicating whether or not the file has been renamed, moved, or deleted since it was first opened.

method.name: fcntl_sync
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_SYNC opcode is generated internally by SQLite and sent to the VFS before the xSync method is invoked on a database file descriptor.

method.name: fcntl_commit_phasetwo
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_COMMIT_PHASETWO opcode is generated internally and sent to the VFS after a transaction has been committed but before the database is unlocked.

method.name: fcntl_win32_set_handle
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_WIN32_SET_HANDLE opcode is used for debugging to swap the file handle with the one pointed to by the argument.

method.name: fcntl_wal_block
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_WAL_BLOCK is a signal to the VFS layer that it might be advantageous to block on the next WAL lock if the lock is not immediately available.

method.name: fcntl_zipvfs
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_ZIPVFS opcode is implemented by zipvfs only. All other VFS should return SQLITE_NOTFOUND for this opcode.

method.name: fcntl_rbu
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_RBU opcode is implemented by the special VFS used by the RBU extension only.

method.name: fcntl_vfs_pointer
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_VFS_POINTER opcode finds a pointer to the top-level VFS currently in use.

method.name: fcntl_journal_pointer
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_JOURNAL_POINTER opcode obtains a pointer to the sqlite3_file object associated with the journal file for a particular database connection.

method.name: fcntl_win32_get_handle
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_WIN32_GET_HANDLE opcode can be used to obtain the underlying native file handle associated with a file handle.

method.name: fcntl_pdb
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_PDB opcode is reserved for future use.

method.name: fcntl_begin_atomic_write
method.description: If the SQLITE_FCNTL_BEGIN_ATOMIC_WRITE opcode returns SQLITE_OK, then the file descriptor is placed in batch write mode for atomic writes.

method.name: fcntl_commit_atomic_write
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_COMMIT_ATOMIC_WRITE opcode causes all write operations since the last begin atomic write to be performed atomically.

method.name: fcntl_rollback_atomic_write
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_ROLLBACK_ATOMIC_WRITE opcode causes all write operations since the last begin atomic write to be rolled back.

method.name: fcntl_lock_timeout
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_LOCK_TIMEOUT opcode configures a VFS to block for up to a specified number of milliseconds before failing when attempting to obtain a file lock.

method.name: fcntl_data_version
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_DATA_VERSION opcode is used to detect changes to a database file via a pointer to a 32-bit unsigned integer.

method.name: fcntl_size_limit
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_SIZE_LIMIT opcode is used by in-memory VFS to set an upper bound on the size of the in-memory database.

method.name: fcntl_ckpt_done
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CKPT_DONE opcode is invoked from within a checkpoint in WAL mode after the client has finished copying pages from the WAL file.

method.name: fcntl_reserve_bytes
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_RESERVE_BYTES opcode is reserved for future use.

method.name: fcntl_ckpt_start
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CKPT_START opcode is invoked from within a checkpoint in WAL mode before the client starts to copy pages from the WAL file.

method.name: fcntl_external_reader
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_EXTERNAL_READER opcode is used to detect whether there is a database client in another process with a WAL-mode transaction open.

method.name: fcntl_cksm_file
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CKSM_FILE opcode is for internal use by the checksum VFS shim only.

method.name: fcntl_reset_cache
method.description: If there is currently no transaction open on the database, the SQLITE_FCNTL_RESET_CACHE file-control purges the contents of the in-memory page cache.

method.name: fcntl_null_io
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_NULL_IO opcode sets the file descriptor so that it will no longer read or write to the database file.

method.name: fcntl_block_on_connect
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_BLOCK_ON_CONNECT opcode is used to configure the VFS to block when taking a SHARED lock to connect to a WAL mode database.

method.name: fcntl_filestat
method.description: The SQLITE_FCNTL_FILESTAT opcode returns low-level diagnostic information about sqlite3_file objects used to access database and journal files.

method.name: get_lockproxyfile
method.description: Deprecated alias for SQLITE_FCNTL_GET_LOCKPROXYFILE.

method.name: set_lockproxyfile
method.description: Deprecated alias for SQLITE_FCNTL_SET_LOCKPROXYFILE.

method.name: last_errno
method.description: Deprecated alias for SQLITE_FCNTL_LAST_ERRNO.

method.name: access_exists
method.description: Checks whether the file exists (for xAccess VFS method).

method.name: access_readwrite
method.description: Checks if a directory is both readable and writable (for xAccess VFS method).

method.name: access_read
method.description: Checks whether the file is readable (for xAccess VFS method).

method.name: shm_unlock
method.description: Unlock operation for shared memory region (for xShmLock VFS method).

method.name: shm_lock
method.description: Lock operation for shared memory region (for xShmLock VFS method).

method.name: shm_shared
method.description: Shared lock flag for shared memory operations (for xShmLock VFS method).

method.name: shm_exclusive
method.description: Exclusive lock flag for shared memory operations (for xShmLock VFS method).

method.name: shm_nlock
method.description: Maximum xShmLock index (offset argument upper bound for locks).

method.name: initialize
method.description: Initializes the SQLite library. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise. Most applications do not need to call this directly unless using custom builds or special initialization requirements.

method.name: shutdown
method.description: Shuts down the SQLite library and releases associated resources. Returns an integer result code.

method.name: os_init
method.description: Initializes the SQLite OS interface.

method.name: os_end
method.description: Ends the operating system interface for SQLite. Should be called before process shutdown.

method.name: config
method.description: Stub for sqlite3_config(). This API is not available from Godot because its variadic arguments cannot be safely mapped. Returns -1 and does nothing.

method.name: db_config
method.description: Configures a database connection using sqlite3_db_config. The 'op' argument is the configuration verb. Extra arguments should be provided in the 'args' array. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. Only up to 3 arguments are supported in 'args'.

method.name: sqlite_config_singlethread
method.description: Sets the threading mode to Single-thread.

method.name: sqlite_config_multithread
method.description: Sets the threading mode to Multi-thread.

method.name: sqlite_config_serialized
method.description: Sets the threading mode to Serialized.

method.name: sqlite_config_malloc
method.description: Specifies alternative memory allocation routines.

method.name: sqlite_config_getmalloc
method.description: Retrieves the current memory allocation routines.

method.name: sqlite_config_scratch
method.description: No longer used.

method.name: sqlite_config_pagecache
method.description: Specifies a memory pool for the database page cache.

method.name: sqlite_config_heap
method.description: Specifies a static memory buffer for dynamic memory allocation.

method.name: sqlite_config_memstatus
method.description: Enables or disables memory allocation statistics collection.

method.name: sqlite_config_mutex
method.description: Specifies alternative mutex routines.

method.name: sqlite_config_getmutex
method.description: Retrieves the current mutex routines.

method.name: sqlite_config_lookaside
method.description: Sets the default size of lookaside memory on each database connection.

method.name: sqlite_config_pcache
method.description: Obsolete option for custom page cache; now a no-op.

method.name: sqlite_config_getpcache
method.description: Obsolete option to retrieve custom page cache; now a no-op.

method.name: sqlite_config_log
method.description: Configures the SQLite global error log.

method.name: sqlite_config_uri
method.description: Globally enables or disables URI handling.

method.name: sqlite_config_pcache2
method.description: Specifies a custom page cache implementation.

method.name: sqlite_config_getpcache2
method.description: Retrieves the current page cache implementation.

method.name: sqlite_config_covering_index_scan
method.description: Enables or disables use of covering indices for full table scans.

method.name: sqlite_config_sqllog
method.description: Configures SQL statement logging (requires SQLITE_ENABLE_SQLLOG).

method.name: sqlite_config_mmap_size
method.description: Sets the default and maximum mmap size limits.

method.name: sqlite_config_win32_heapsize
method.description: Sets the maximum heap size on Windows (requires SQLITE_WIN32_MALLOC).

method.name: sqlite_config_pcache_hdrsz
method.description: Retrieves the number of extra bytes per page required for page cache.

method.name: sqlite_config_pmasz
method.description: Sets the minimum PMA size for the multithreaded sorter.

method.name: sqlite_config_stmtjrnl_spill
method.description: Sets the statement journal spill-to-disk threshold.

method.name: sqlite_config_small_malloc
method.description: Provides a hint to avoid large memory allocations if possible.

method.name: sqlite_config_sorterref_size
method.description: Sets the sorter-reference size threshold.

method.name: sqlite_config_memdb_maxsize
method.description: Sets the default maximum size for an in-memory database.

method.name: sqlite_config_rowid_in_view
method.description: Enables or disables the ability for VIEWs to have a ROWID.

method.name: dbconfig_maindbname
method.description: Configuration option to change the name of the 'main' database schema. Argument is a const char* with the new name.

method.name: dbconfig_lookaside
method.description: Configuration option to adjust the lookaside memory allocator. Arguments are (void* buf, int sz, int cnt).

method.name: dbconfig_enable_fkey
method.description: Enables or disables enforcement of foreign key constraints. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_trigger
method.description: Enables or disables CREATE TRIGGER support. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_fts3_tokenizer
method.description: Enables or disables use of fts3_tokenizer() function without bound parameters. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_load_extension
method.description: Enables or disables the sqlite3_load_extension() C-API interface. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_no_ckpt_on_close
method.description: Disables or enables checkpoints when closing the last connection in WAL mode. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_qpsg
method.description: Activates or deactivates the query planner stability guarantee (QPSG). Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_trigger_eqp
method.description: Enables or disables EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output for trigger programs. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_reset_database
method.description: Sets the RESET_DATABASE flag to reset a database to empty. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_defensive
method.description: Activates or deactivates the defensive flag, disabling features that allow deliberate corruption. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_writable_schema
method.description: Activates or deactivates the writable_schema flag. Equivalent to PRAGMA writable_schema. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_legacy_alter_table
method.description: Activates or deactivates legacy ALTER TABLE RENAME behavior. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dbconfig_dqs_dml
method.description: Activates or deactivates legacy double-quoted string literal support for DML statements. Arguments: int on/off, int* result.

method.name: dqs_ddl
method.description: Database config option code for DQS_DDL.

method.name: enable_view
method.description: Database config option code for ENABLE_VIEW.

method.name: legacy_file_format
method.description: Database config option code for LEGACY_FILE_FORMAT.

method.name: trusted_schema
method.description: Database config option code for TRUSTED_SCHEMA.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus
method.description: Database config option code for STMT_SCANSTATUS.

method.name: reverse_scanorder
method.description: Database config option code for REVERSE_SCANORDER.

method.name: enable_attach_create
method.description: Database config option code for ENABLE_ATTACH_CREATE.

method.name: enable_attach_write
method.description: Database config option code for ENABLE_ATTACH_WRITE.

method.name: enable_comments
method.description: Database config option code for ENABLE_COMMENTS.

method.name: fp_digits
method.description: Database config option code for FP_DIGITS.

method.name: max
method.description: Largest database config option code.

method.name: extended_result_codes
method.description: Enables or disables the extended result codes feature of SQLite for the given database handle. Returns an SQLite error code.

method.name: changes64
method.description: Returns the number of database rows that were changed, inserted, or deleted by the most recent SQL statement.

method.name: total_changes64
method.description: Returns the total number of changes made to the database by INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statements since the database connection was opened.

method.name: is_interrupted
method.description: Returns nonzero if the supplied SQLite connection is currently interrupted.

method.name: complete16
method.description: Checks whether the supplied UTF-16 SQL statement is complete.

method.name: busy_handler
method.description: Registers a callback to handle SQLITE_BUSY errors for the given database connection. The handler will be called with the invocation count for the current locking event. Returning 0 from the handler stops further attempts and causes SQLITE_BUSY to be returned; returning a nonzero value causes SQLite to retry. Passing an invalid or null Callable clears the busy handler. Only one busy handler can be set at a time. Setting a new handler replaces the previous one.

method.name: free_table
method.description: Frees memory previously allocated by sqlite3_get_table(). Pass the pointer returned by sqlite3_get_table().

method.name: mprintf
method.description: Formats a string using SQLite's printf-style formatting with the given format string. Note: This wrapper only supports format strings with no additional arguments.

method.name: vmprintf
method.description: UNSUPPORTED: This function cannot be used from Godot because passing va_list is not supported. Always returns an empty string.

method.name: malloc
method.description: Allocates a block of memory at least n_bytes in length using SQLite's internal memory allocator. Returns the pointer as an int64_t, or 0 if allocation fails. The returned pointer must be freed using the appropriate SQLite memory free function.

method.name: malloc64
method.description: Allocates a block of memory of the specified size (in bytes) and returns a pointer as an integer handle. The returned pointer must be freed with sqlite3_free(). Returns 0 if allocation fails or if size is negative.

method.name: realloc
method.description: Reallocates a memory block, possibly moving it. Returns a pointer to the newly allocated memory, or 0 if allocation fails.

method.name: realloc64
method.description: Reallocates a memory block using SQLite's allocator. Returns a pointer to the new memory block or 0 if allocation fails.

method.name: msize
method.description: Returns the size of a memory allocation in bytes for a pointer previously obtained from SQLite's memory allocator.

method.name: memory_used
method.description: Returns the number of bytes of memory currently outstanding (malloced but not freed) by SQLite. The value includes any overhead added by SQLite in its implementation of memory allocation routines, but not overhead added by underlying system libraries.

method.name: memory_highwater
method.description: Returns the maximum amount of memory ever used by SQLite, optionally resetting the highwater mark if reset_flag is non-zero.

method.name: randomness
method.description: Fills and returns a buffer of n random bytes using SQLite's internal PRNG. If n <= 0, reseeds the PRNG and returns an empty array.

method.name: set_authorizer
method.description: Registers an authorizer callback for the given SQLite database connection. The callback is invoked during SQL statement compilation to determine if specific actions are allowed. The callable receives the action code and up to four optional string parameters describing the action. Return SQLITE_OK to allow, SQLITE_IGNORE to partially allow, or SQLITE_DENY to reject.

method.name: trace
method.description: DEPRECATED. Registers a trace callback for SQL statement execution on the given database. The trace_callback is called with a String containing the SQL text as each statement begins executing. Returns an opaque pointer as an integer, used to unregister the callback. Passing an invalid Callable disables tracing. See sqlite3_trace() documentation for details. Note: This function is deprecated in SQLite. Prefer sqlite3_trace_v2().

method.name: trace_v2
method.description: Registers a trace callback function for the SQLite database connection. The callback is invoked for trace events specified by the mask. The callback receives (type, pArg1, pArg2) as arguments. Passing an invalid callback or zero mask disables tracing.

method.name: uri_parameter
method.description: Returns the value of a query parameter from a SQLite database URI filename, or an empty String if not found.

method.name: uri_boolean
method.description: Returns the value of the URI parameter named z_param as a boolean. If the parameter is not present, returns b_default.

method.name: uri_int64
method.description: Returns the 64-bit integer value of the specified URI parameter, or the provided default value if not found.

method.name: uri_key
method.description: Returns the key at index index from the URI filename string z, or an empty string if index is out of range.

method.name: filename_database
method.description: Returns the corresponding database filename for the given SQLite database, journal, or WAL filename. If the input is not valid, the result is undefined.

method.name: filename_journal
method.description: Returns the name of the journal file associated with the given filename.

method.name: filename_wal
method.description: Returns the name of the WAL file for the given database filename.

method.name: database_file_object
method.description: For a journal or WAL file name previously passed to sqlite3_vfs.xOpen, returns a handle to the associated main database file. Intended for use in custom VFS implementations only. Returns null if the input is not valid for this function.

method.name: create_filename
method.description: Creates a new SQLite VFS filename handle using the specified database, journal, WAL filenames, and key/value URI parameters. The returned handle must be freed using sqlite3_free_filename.

method.name: free_filename
method.description: Frees a filename string previously allocated by SQLite. Pass the pointer as an int64_t obtained from SQLite filename allocation functions. Do not pass a Godot String.

method.name: errcode
method.description: Returns the numeric result code or extended result code for the most recent failed SQLite API call associated with the given database connection. The error code may change after subsequent API calls.

method.name: extended_errcode
method.description: Returns the extended error code for the given SQLite database connection.

method.name: errmsg
method.description: Returns the error message string for the most recent SQLite operation on the given database handle.

method.name: errstr
method.description: Returns the English-language string that describes an SQLite error code.

method.name: limit
method.description: Sets or queries a run-time limit for the specified database connection. Returns the previous value of the limit. Pass -1 as new_value to query without changing.

method.name: expanded_sql
method.description: Returns the SQL text of the prepared statement with bound parameters expanded as a String. Returns an empty string if the operation fails.

method.name: bind_blob
method.description: Binds a binary blob value to the specified parameter of a prepared SQLite statement. The parameter index is 1-based. The data is copied by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT). Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an SQLite error code.

method.name: bind_blob64
method.description: Binds a blob value to the specified parameter index in the prepared statement. The blob data is provided as a PackedByteArray. The destructor argument should be -1 for SQLITE_TRANSIENT (SQLite will copy the data) or 0 for SQLITE_STATIC (data is assumed to remain valid for the statement's lifetime). Returns a SQLite result code. Other values for destructor are unsupported and unsafe.

method.name: bind_int64
method.description: Binds a 64-bit integer value to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

method.name: bind_zeroblob
method.description: Binds a zeroblob of length n to the parameter at the given index of the prepared statement.

method.name: column_name16
method.description: Returns the UTF-16 name of the N-th column in the result set of the prepared statement, or an empty string if N is out of range.

method.name: column_database_name16
method.description: Returns the database name for the N-th column of the result set of the prepared statement, as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty string if unavailable.

method.name: column_table_name
method.description: Returns the name of the table for the specified column in the prepared statement. Returns an empty string if the information is not available or the column does not come from a table.

method.name: column_decltype16
method.description: Returns the declared type of the column as a UTF-16 string, or an empty string if unavailable.

method.name: sqlite_text
method.description: Returns the SQLite type code for text (SQLITE_TEXT).

method.name: sqlite3_text
method.description: Returns the value of the SQLITE3_TEXT constant.

method.name: column_blob
method.description: Returns the BLOB data of the specified column in the current result row as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty array if the column is not a BLOB or if the value is NULL.

method.name: column_double
method.description: Returns the value of the specified column as a double.

method.name: column_int
method.description: Returns the value of the column at index i_col in the current row of the result set of the prepared statement as an integer.

method.name: column_int64
method.description: Returns the value of column i_col in the current row of the result set as a 64-bit integer.

method.name: value_blob
method.description: Returns the BLOB value of the provided sqlite3_value as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty array if the value is not of BLOB type or is NULL.

method.name: value_double
method.description: Returns the value as a double.

method.name: value_int
method.description: Extracts the integer value from the given sqlite3_value handle.

method.name: value_int64
method.description: Returns the value as a 64-bit integer.

method.name: value_pointer
method.description: Returns the pointer stored in the sqlite3_value for the given type name, or null if not set.

method.name: value_bytes
method.description: Returns the number of bytes in the value for the given SQLite value handle.

method.name: value_free
method.description: Frees/releases the given SQLite value object. After this call, the handle is invalid.

method.name: set_auxdata
method.description: Sets auxiliary data for the given context and parameter index. The 'auxdata' Variant is passed to SQLite, and the 'destructor' Callable will be called with the auxdata when SQLite destroys it.

method.name: set_clientdata
method.description: Associates arbitrary client data with a SQLite database connection and a key. The client data pointer and cleanup callback pointer are passed as int64_t values representing the C pointers. Returns the SQLite result code.

method.name: static_destructor_type
method.description: Special value for sqlite3_destructor_type indicating that the content is static and does not need to be freed.

method.name: transient_destructor_type
method.description: Special value for sqlite3_destructor_type indicating that SQLite will make its own private copy of the content.

method.name: result_blob
method.description: Sets the result of an application-defined SQL function to a BLOB value. If is_transient is true, SQLite will make a private copy of the data; if false, SQLite assumes the data pointer remains valid for its use.

method.name: result_blob64
method.description: Sets the result of an SQLite function to a BLOB value using the supplied data. The destructor parameter should be either SQLITE_TRANSIENT (to copy the data) or SQLITE_STATIC (data must remain valid for SQLite's use).

method.name: result_double
method.description: Sets the result of an SQLite user-defined function to the given double value.

method.name: result_error16
method.description: Sets the error message for the context using a UTF-16 encoded string. The error_message is converted to UTF-16 and passed to SQLite. If nbytes < 0, the message is treated as null-terminated. If nbytes >= 0, only nbytes bytes are read.

method.name: result_error_nomem
method.description: Signals an out-of-memory error for the current SQL function context.

method.name: result_value
method.description: Sets the result of an SQL function to the value of another sqlite3_value.

method.name: create_collation_v2
method.description: Creates a new collation for the SQLite database. The 'compare' Callable will be called to compare two strings as part of the collation. The 'destroy' Callable (if valid) will be called when the collation is destroyed. The compare Callable should accept two PackedByteArray arguments and return an int (negative, zero, or positive).

method.name: collation_needed16
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked when a UTF-16 collation sequence is needed but not yet loaded. The callback is called with (db: Ref<Sqlite3Handle>, eTextRep: int, collation_name: String).

method.name: get_temp_directory
method.description: Gets the current folder used for SQLite temporary files, or an empty string if unset. Use with caution: this is a global variable and is not thread-safe. Changing it after SQLite has been initialized or from multiple threads is unsafe.

method.name: set_temp_directory
method.description: Sets the folder used for SQLite temporary files. Passing an empty string unsets the directory. This is a global variable and is not thread-safe; only set during process initialization and before any SQLite API calls. The string is copied and ownership is managed appropriately.

method.name: get_data_directory
method.description: Returns the directory name used by SQLite for database files with relative paths. If null, files are relative to the process's current directory. Only used by the Windows VFS; ignored by the Unix VFS. Not thread-safe. See C API docs for memory management caveats.

method.name: set_data_directory
method.description: Sets the directory name used by SQLite for database files with relative paths. Only used by the Windows VFS; ignored by the Unix VFS. Not thread-safe. Memory pointed to by this variable may be freed by SQLite if certain pragmas are used; allocate only with malloc or SQLite's allocator. Changing this while a database is open may corrupt the database. See SQLite documentation for details.

method.name: win32_set_directory
method.description: Win32-only interface: Sets the value associated with the sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory variable, depending on the type. Passing an empty string for z_value will reset (free) the previous value. The function returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_ERROR if the type is unsupported, or SQLITE_NOMEM if memory could not be allocated.

method.name: sqlite3_win32_set_directory8
method.description: Sets a Win32 directory used by SQLite for temporary or database files. The 'type' parameter selects which directory is set. Returns a result code.

method.name: win32_set_directory16
method.description: Sets a Windows directory used by SQLite, specifying the type and the directory as a UTF-16 string. Returns a status code.

method.name: win32_data_directory_type
method.description: Macro for the Win32 data directory type, for use with sqlite3_win32_set_directory.

method.name: win32_temp_directory_type
method.description: Macro for the Win32 temp directory type, for use with sqlite3_win32_set_directory.

method.name: get_autocommit
method.description: Returns non-zero if the given database connection is in autocommit mode, zero otherwise. Autocommit mode is on by default, disabled by BEGIN, and re-enabled by COMMIT or ROLLBACK. Use this function to determine if a transaction was automatically rolled back after an error. The return value is undefined if another thread changes autocommit status during the call.

method.name: next_stmt
method.description: Returns the next prepared statement after 'stmt' associated with the database connection 'db'. If 'stmt' is null, returns the first prepared statement. Returns null if there are no more prepared statements.

method.name: rollback_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked when a transaction is rolled back on the given database connection. The callback receives no arguments. Returns the previous user data pointer as int64_t (only meaningful if you chain hooks). Warning: The callback will be called from the thread performing the rollback; avoid blocking or accessing freed objects.

method.name: release_memory
method.description: Attempts to free up to N bytes of heap memory used by SQLite for non-essential allocations. Returns the number of bytes actually freed, which may be more or less than requested. Returns zero if memory management is not enabled.

method.name: db_release_memory
method.description: Attempts to free as much heap memory as possible from the given SQLite database connection.

method.name: hard_heap_limit64
method.description: Sets or queries the hard heap memory limit for SQLite in bytes. If n > 0, sets the limit to n and returns the previous value. If n == 0, returns the current limit.

method.name: soft_heap_limit
method.description: DEPRECATED. Sets the soft heap limit for SQLite using the deprecated interface. Provided for historical compatibility only. New applications should use soft_heap_limit64 instead.

method.name: load_extension
method.description: Loads an SQLite extension library from the specified file. Optionally specify the entry point with 'proc' (empty for default). Returns a Dictionary with 'result' (int) and 'error_message' (String, empty if no error).

method.name: cancel_auto_extension
method.description: Unregisters the initialization routine previously registered using sqlite3_auto_extension. The argument x_entry_point must be a pointer to a function with signature void(void), cast to int64_t. Returns 1 if successfully unregistered, 0 otherwise.

method.name: reset_auto_extension
method.description: Disables all automatic extensions previously registered using sqlite3_auto_extension().

method.name: sqlite_index_scan_unique
method.description: Scan visits at most 1 row.

method.name: sqlite_index_scan_hex
method.description: Display idxNum as hex in EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_eq
method.description: Constraint operator: equals (=).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_gt
method.description: Constraint operator: greater than (>).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_le
method.description: Constraint operator: less than or equal (<=).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_lt
method.description: Constraint operator: less than (<).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_ge
method.description: Constraint operator: greater than or equal (>=).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_match
method.description: Constraint operator: MATCH.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_like
method.description: Constraint operator: LIKE.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_glob
method.description: Constraint operator: GLOB.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_regexp
method.description: Constraint operator: REGEXP.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_ne
method.description: Constraint operator: not equals (!=).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_isnot
method.description: Constraint operator: IS NOT.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_isnotnull
method.description: Constraint operator: IS NOT NULL.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_isnull
method.description: Constraint operator: IS NULL.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_is
method.description: Constraint operator: IS.

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_limit
method.description: Constraint operator: LIMIT (no left-hand operand).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_offset
method.description: Constraint operator: OFFSET (no left-hand operand).

method.name: sqlite_index_constraint_function
method.description: Constraint operator: function (reserved for xFindFunction overloads).

method.name: create_module
method.description: Registers a new virtual table module with the specified name on the given SQLite database connection. If the module is null, any existing module with the same name is dropped. The client_data pointer is passed to the virtual table implementation.

method.name: blob_open
method.description: Opens a BLOB handle for incremental I/O on a specific row, column, and table. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_blob to the opened blob handle.

method.name: vfs_register
method.description: Registers a virtual file system (VFS) with SQLite's global list. If make_dflt is non-zero, sets this VFS as the default.

method.name: vfs_unregister
method.description: Unregisters the given VFS object.

method.name: mutex_free
method.description: Frees an SQLite mutex object. After this call, the mutex handle is no longer valid.

method.name: mutex_enter
method.description: Enters (locks) the specified SQLite mutex.

method.name: mutex_try
method.description: Attempts to acquire the mutex without blocking. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: mutex_leave
method.description: Releases the mutex.

method.name: db_mutex
method.description: Retrieves the mutex object that serializes access to the given SQLite database connection. Returns null if the threading mode is not serialized.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_first
method.description: Operation code for sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_prng_save
method.description: Operation code for PRNG save in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_prng_restore
method.description: Operation code for PRNG restore in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_prng_reset
method.description: Operation code for PRNG reset in sqlite3_test_control(). Not used.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_fk_no_action
method.description: Operation code for foreign key no action in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_bitvec_test
method.description: Operation code for BitVec test in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_fault_install
method.description: Operation code for fault install in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_benign_malloc_hooks
method.description: Operation code for benign malloc hooks in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_pending_byte
method.description: Operation code for pending byte in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_assert
method.description: Operation code for assert in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_always
method.description: Operation code for always in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_reserve
method.description: Operation code for reserve in sqlite3_test_control(). Not used.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_json_selfcheck
method.description: Operation code for JSON selfcheck in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_optimizations
method.description: Operation code for optimizations in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_iskeyword
method.description: Operation code for iskeyword in sqlite3_test_control(). Not used.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_getopt
method.description: Operation code for getopt in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_scratchmalloc
method.description: Operation code for scratchmalloc in sqlite3_test_control(). Not used.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_internal_functions
method.description: Operation code for internal functions in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_localtime_fault
method.description: Operation code for localtime fault in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_explain_stmt
method.description: Operation code for explain statement in sqlite3_test_control(). Not used.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_once_reset_threshold
method.description: Operation code for once reset threshold in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_never_corrupt
method.description: Operation code for never corrupt in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_vdbe_coverage
method.description: Operation code for VDBE coverage in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_byteorder
method.description: Operation code for byteorder in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_isinit
method.description: Operation code for isinit in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_sorter_mmap
method.description: Operation code for sorter mmap in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_imposter
method.description: Operation code for imposter in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_parser_coverage
method.description: Operation code for parser coverage in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_result_intreal
method.description: Operation code for result intreal in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_prng_seed
method.description: Operation code for PRNG seed in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_extra_schema_checks
method.description: Operation code for extra schema checks in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_seek_count
method.description: Operation code for seek count in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_traceflags
method.description: Operation code for traceflags in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_tune
method.description: Operation code for tune in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_logest
method.description: Operation code for logest in sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_uselongdouble
method.description: Operation code for uselongdouble in sqlite3_test_control(). Not used.

method.name: sqlite_testctrl_last
method.description: Largest operation code for sqlite3_test_control(). See SQLite testing interface operation codes.

method.name: keyword_count
method.description: Returns the number of distinct SQL keywords recognized by SQLite.

method.name: keyword_name
method.description: Retrieves the name of the SQLite keyword at the given index. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'name' (String), 'length' (int), and 'result' (int, the function's return code).

method.name: keyword_check
method.description: Checks if the given word is an SQL keyword. Returns nonzero if the word is a keyword, zero otherwise.

method.name: str_new
method.description: Allocates and initializes a new dynamic string object for SQLite. The returned handle must later be finalized with str_finish().

method.name: str_free
method.description: Frees a sqlite3_str object and releases any resources held by it.

method.name: str_append
method.description: Appends up to N bytes from the input string to the sqlite3 string accumulator. If N is negative, the entire string up to the first null terminator is appended.

method.name: str_appendall
method.description: Appends all of z_in to the sqlite3_str object.

method.name: str_appendchar
method.description: Appends the character c to the string buffer str n times.

method.name: str_reset
method.description: Resets the state of the SQLite string object.

method.name: str_truncate
method.description: Truncates the string builder to at most n bytes.

method.name: str_length
method.description: Returns the current length of the SQLite dynamic string.

method.name: str_value
method.description: Returns the current contents of the sqlite3_str object as a String.

method.name: status64
method.description: Retrieves status parameters for the SQLite library (64-bit values). Returns a Dictionary with error code, current value, and highwater value.

method.name: sqlite_status_memory_used
method.description: This parameter is the current amount of memory checked out using sqlite3_malloc(), either directly or indirectly.

method.name: sqlite_status_pagecache_used
method.description: This parameter returns the number of pages used out of the pagecache memory allocator configured using SQLITE_CONFIG_PAGECACHE. The value returned is in pages, not in bytes.

method.name: sqlite_status_pagecache_overflow
method.description: This parameter returns the number of bytes of page cache allocation which could not be satisfied by the SQLITE_CONFIG_PAGECACHE buffer and were forced to overflow to sqlite3_malloc().

method.name: sqlite_status_scratch_used
method.description: No longer used.

method.name: sqlite_status_scratch_overflow
method.description: No longer used.

method.name: sqlite_status_malloc_size
method.description: This parameter records the largest memory allocation request handed to sqlite3_malloc() or sqlite3_realloc(). Only the value returned in the *pHighwater parameter to sqlite3_status() is of interest.

method.name: sqlite_status_parser_stack
method.description: The *pHighwater parameter records the deepest parser stack. The *pHighwater value is only meaningful if SQLite is compiled with YYTRACKMAXSTACKDEPTH.

method.name: sqlite_status_pagecache_size
method.description: This parameter records the largest memory allocation request handed to the pagecache memory allocator. Only the value returned in the *pHighwater parameter to sqlite3_status() is of interest.

method.name: sqlite_status_scratch_size
method.description: No longer used.

method.name: sqlite_status_malloc_count
method.description: This parameter records the number of separate memory allocations currently checked out.

method.name: db_status
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information about a single SQLite database connection. Returns a Dictionary containing the error code, the current value, and the highest instantaneous value for the requested status parameter. If reset_flg is true, the highest value is reset to the current value.

method.name: db_status64
method.description: Retrieves 64-bit current and highwater status values for the given SQLite database handle. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (status code), 'current' (current value), and 'highwater' (highwater value).

method.name: stmt_status
method.description: Retrieves the value of a specific status counter for the given prepared statement. If reset_flag is true, the counter is reset to zero after the call.

method.name: backup_step
method.description: Copies up to n_page pages from the source database to the backup. Returns the SQLite status code.

method.name: backup_finish
method.description: Finalizes an SQLite backup operation and releases associated resources. Returns the SQLite result code.

method.name: backup_remaining
method.description: Returns the number of pages still to be backed up in the specified backup object.

method.name: strnicmp
method.description: Compares two strings case-insensitively for the first n bytes. Returns 0 if they are equal, <0 if s1 < s2, >0 if s1 > s2.

method.name: strglob
method.description: Returns 0 if 'str' matches the glob pattern 'glob' using SQLite's case-sensitive GLOB rules. Returns non-zero if there is no match. The matching semantics are identical to the SQL 'X GLOB P' operator in SQLite.

method.name: strlike
method.description: Returns zero if and only if the input string matches the LIKE pattern using the specified escape character, following SQLite's case-insensitive ASCII LIKE rules. Returns non-zero if not matched. For no ESCAPE clause, set escape_char to 0.

method.name: log
method.description: Writes a formatted message to the SQLite error log. The message is created by formatting 'format' with 'args', then logged using the given error code.

method.name: wal_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked each time data is committed to a database in WAL mode. The callback receives (db, db_name, n_pages, user_data) and should return an int. Passing an invalid Callable disables the hook. Returns the previous user data pointer as an integer.

method.name: vtab_config
method.description: Configures the virtual table interface for a database connection using sqlite3_vtab_config. The meaning and number of arguments after 'op' depend on the value of 'op'. Only 0, 1, or 2 additional arguments are supported; each must be an int or String as required by the SQLite API and 'op' value. See SQLite's documentation for the valid combinations.

method.name: vtab_on_conflict
method.description: Returns the ON CONFLICT policy in effect for a virtual table xUpdate operation. Call only from within a virtual table xUpdate method for INSERT or UPDATE.

method.name: vtab_in_next
method.description: Advances to the next value in a virtual table IN operator iteration. On success, sets out_pp_out to the next value.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_int
method.description: Retrieves integer scan status information for a prepared statement using sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int) and 'value' (int64_t). Only available if compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_double
method.description: Retrieves double-precision scan status information for a prepared statement using sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int) and 'value' (double). Only available if compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_string
method.description: Retrieves string scan status information for a prepared statement using sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int) and 'value' (String). Only available if compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS.

method.name: scanstat_complex
method.description: Prepared statement scan status flag: SQLITE_SCANSTAT_COMPLEX.

method.name: snapshot_get
method.description: Attempts to make a new snapshot object recording the current state of the specified schema in the database. On success, writes a snapshot handle to out_snapshot and returns SQLITE_OK. Only available when compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT.

method.name: snapshot_free
method.description: Destroys a sqlite3_snapshot object. The application must eventually free every sqlite3_snapshot object using this routine to avoid a memory leak. Only available when compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT.

method.name: deserialize
method.description: Deserializes a database from the given buffer and attaches it to the specified database connection. Only the SQLITE_DESERIALIZE_READONLY flag is supported by this wrapper, because the buffer is a temporary copy and will not remain alive after this call. Passing other flags is not supported and will return SQLITE_MISUSE. The schema argument specifies which database to reopen (pass an empty string for the main database). See SQLite documentation for detailed semantics and restrictions. This function is unavailable if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_OMIT_DESERIALIZE.

method.name: carray_bind_v2
method.description: Binds an array value to the first argument of the carray() table-valued function in SQLite. 'stmt' is the prepared statement handle. 'index' is the parameter index to bind. 'data' is the array to bind, and 'n_elements' is the number of elements in the array. 'flags' specifies the type of the array (one of SQLITE_CARRAY_INT32, SQLITE_CARRAY_INT64, SQLITE_CARRAY_DOUBLE, SQLITE_CARRAY_TEXT, or SQLITE_CARRAY_BLOB). 'x_del' should be SQLITE_STATIC or SQLITE_TRANSIENT to control the lifetime of the data (custom destructors are not supported; pass as integer constant). 'p_del' is ignored unless a custom destructor is used. Returns the result code from sqlite3_carray_bind_v2.

method.name: rtree_geometry_callback
method.description: Registers a geometry callback named z_geom for use in R-Tree queries. The x_geom Callable will be called during geometry queries with arguments (Sqlite3RtreeGeometryHandle, PackedFloat64Array). It should return either an int SQLite result code, or a Dictionary with 'result_code' and optionally 'output' for the output parameter.

method.name: rtree_query_callback
method.description: Registers a 2nd-generation geometry callback for use in SQLite R-Tree queries. The callback receives a Sqlite3RtreeQueryInfoHandle and the optional context. Returning a non-zero integer from the callback will affect query evaluation. The destructor Callable is called when the context is destroyed.

method.name: not_within
method.description: Object completely outside of query region.

method.name: partly_within
method.description: Object partially overlaps query region.

method.name: fully_within
method.description: Object fully contained within query region.

method.name: session_create
method.description: Creates a new session object attached to the given SQLite database handle. The session object will be used to create changesets for tables in the specified database (z_db, e.g. "main"). On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_session to the new session. On error, returns an SQLite error code and out_session is not modified. Session objects should be deleted before closing the database handle they are attached to. It is not possible to use pre-update hooks with a database while session objects are attached.

method.name: session_enable
method.description: Enable or disable the recording of changes by a session object. When enabled, a session object records changes made to the database. When disabled, it does not. Passing zero disables the session, a value greater than zero enables it, and a value less than zero is a no-op and can be used to query the current state. Returns 1 if the session is enabled or 0 if disabled.

method.name: session_memory_used
method.description: Returns the total amount of heap memory in bytes currently used by the given session object.

method.name: changeset_start_v2
method.description: Starts a new changeset iterator for the given changeset blob. On success, writes the new iterator handle to out_iter. Returns a result code.

method.name: next
method.description: Advances the changeset iterator to the next change. Returns SQLITE_ROW if a valid change is available, SQLITE_DONE if all changes have been visited, or an SQLite error code on failure.

method.name: changeset_concat
method.description: Concatenates two changeset objects. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (the SQLite status code) and 'changeset' (the resulting changeset as a PackedByteArray, or empty if an error occurred). The result is equivalent to applying changeset A followed by changeset B.

method.name: create
method.description: Creates a new changegroup object for combining changesets or patchsets. Returns the new object on success, or null on failure.

method.name: changegroup_delete
method.description: Delete a Changegroup Object. Destructor for sqlite3_changegroup.

method.name: rebaser_create
method.description: Allocates a new changeset rebaser object. If successful, sets out_handle to the new object and returns SQLITE_OK. Otherwise, returns an SQLite error code and out_handle is not modified.

method.name: configure
method.description: Configure the changeset rebaser object to rebase changesets according to the conflict resolutions described by rebase_buffer, which must have been obtained from a previous call to sqlite3changeset_apply_v2(). EXPERIMENTAL.

method.name: rebaser_delete
method.description: Deletes a changeset rebaser object and frees all associated resources. Call once per object created with sqlite3rebaser_create().

method.name: changeset_concat_strm
method.description: Concatenates two changeset input streams into a single output stream using user-provided Godot Callables as I/O callbacks. Each input and output Callable receives a PackedByteArray buffer and a user context Variant. Input callbacks should return a Dictionary with keys 'data' (PackedByteArray), 'size' (int), and optionally 'result' (int). Output callback should return an int status code.

method.name: changeset_start_v2_strm
method.description: Starts a changeset iterator from a stream using a custom input callback. The x_input callback receives one argument (requested buffer size) and must return a PackedByteArray (the data to provide). It may alternatively return an Array [PackedByteArray, int] to specify a custom result code. On success, out_iter is set to the new iterator.

method.name: changeset_strm
method.description: Writes a changeset for the given session to a stream by invoking the provided callback for each output chunk. The callback receives a PackedByteArray containing the data buffer and must return an int status. Returning non-zero from the callback will stop streaming and return that value.

method.name: patchset_strm
method.description: Streams a patchset for the given session by invoking the provided output callback (`x_output`) as needed. The callback receives two arguments: the user data (`p_out`) and a PackedByteArray containing the data chunk. Returns an SQLite result code.

method.name: changegroup_add_strm
method.description: Adds changes from a stream to the changegroup. The x_input Callable receives an int buffer size and must return a PackedByteArray with data to copy. Returns 0 on success, or an error code.

method.name: session_config_strmsize
method.description: Value for sqlite3session_config() representing STRMSIZE.

method.name: close_v2
method.description: Closes the database connection. After this call, the database handle is no longer valid. Returns an SQLite status code.

method.name: exec
method.description: Executes one or more SQL statements in a single call. Optionally calls the callback for each result row. Returns a Dictionary with 'code' (SQLite result code) and 'error_message' (if any).

method.name: last_insert_rowid
method.description: Returns the ROWID of the most recent successful INSERT into a rowid table for the given database connection. Returns 0 if no successful INSERTs have occurred.

method.name: set_last_insert_rowid
method.description: Sets the value returned by sqlite3_last_insert_rowid() for the given database handle to the specified rowid, without inserting a row into the database.

method.name: changes
method.description: Returns the number of rows modified, inserted, or deleted by the most recent INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statement on the given database connection. Only changes to real tables are counted; changes due to triggers or views are not included.

method.name: total_changes
method.description: Returns the total number of rows inserted, modified, or deleted by all INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statements executed on this database connection since it was opened. See SQLite documentation for details.

method.name: complete
method.description: Determines if the given SQL string forms a complete SQL statement. Returns 1 if complete, 0 if incomplete, or SQLITE_NOMEM on memory allocation error.

method.name: setlk_timeout
method.description: Sets the setlk timeout (in milliseconds) for blocking locks on WAL-mode databases for the specified database handle. Only useful if built with SQLITE_ENABLE_SETLK_TIMEOUT and if the VFS supports blocking locks. Passing 0 disables blocking locks; passing -1 requests to block indefinitely. Call sqlite3_busy_timeout() first to set the busy timeout separately if desired. The flags parameter may include SQLITE_SETLK_BLOCK_ON_CONNECT.

method.name: setlk_block_on_connect
method.description: Flags for sqlite3_setlk_timeout().

method.name: get_table
method.description: Evaluates one or more semicolon-separated SQL statements and returns a result table as a PackedStringArray, along with the row count, column count, error message, and result code. The result table is a flat array: the first N columns are column names, followed by row results. This is a legacy interface preserved for backwards compatibility.

method.name: sqlite_deny
method.description: Abort the SQL statement with an error.

method.name: sqlite_ignore
method.description: Don't allow access, but don't generate an error.

method.name: sqlite_create_index
method.description: Authorize creation of an index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_create_table
method.description: Authorize creation of a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_create_temp_index
method.description: Authorize creation of a temporary index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_create_temp_table
method.description: Authorize creation of a temporary table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_create_temp_trigger
method.description: Authorize creation of a temporary trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_create_temp_view
method.description: Authorize creation of a temporary view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_create_trigger
method.description: Authorize creation of a trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_create_view
method.description: Authorize creation of a view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_delete
method.description: Authorize deletion from a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_drop_index
method.description: Authorize dropping an index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_drop_table
method.description: Authorize dropping a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_drop_temp_index
method.description: Authorize dropping a temporary index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_drop_temp_table
method.description: Authorize dropping a temporary table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_drop_temp_trigger
method.description: Authorize dropping a temporary trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_drop_temp_view
method.description: Authorize dropping a temporary view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_drop_trigger
method.description: Authorize dropping a trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_drop_view
