method.description: Authorize dropping a view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_insert
method.description: Authorize inserting into a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_pragma
method.description: Authorize PRAGMA statement execution. 3rd arg: Pragma Name, 4th arg: 1st arg or NULL.

method.name: sqlite_read
method.description: Authorize reading from a table column. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Column Name.

method.name: sqlite_select
method.description: Authorize SELECT statement. 3rd arg: NULL, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_transaction
method.description: Authorize transaction operations. 3rd arg: Operation, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_update
method.description: Authorize updating a table column. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Column Name.

method.name: sqlite_attach
method.description: Authorize ATTACH statement. 3rd arg: Filename, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_detach
method.description: Authorize DETACH statement. 3rd arg: Database Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_alter_table
method.description: Authorize ALTER TABLE statement. 3rd arg: Database Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

method.name: sqlite_reindex
method.description: Authorize REINDEX statement. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_analyze
method.description: Authorize ANALYZE statement. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: sqlite_create_vtable
method.description: Authorize creation of a virtual table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Module Name.

method.name: sqlite_drop_vtable
method.description: Authorize dropping a virtual table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Module Name.

method.name: sqlite_function
method.description: Authorize use of a function. 3rd arg: NULL, 4th arg: Function Name.

method.name: sqlite_savepoint
method.description: Authorize SAVEPOINT operations. 3rd arg: Operation, 4th arg: Savepoint Name.

method.name: sqlite_copy
method.description: No longer used. Deprecated.

method.name: sqlite_recursive
method.description: Authorize recursive trigger or view. 3rd arg: NULL, 4th arg: NULL.

method.name: profile
method.description: DEPRECATED. Installs a profile callback for the given database handle. The callback will be invoked after execution of each SQL statement, receiving the SQL string and elapsed time in nanoseconds. Returns the previous callback pointer as an integer. This API is deprecated in SQLite.

method.name: trace_stmt
method.description: Event code for when a prepared statement begins running.

method.name: trace_profile
method.description: Event code for when a prepared statement finishes running, with profiling information.

method.name: trace_row
method.description: Event code for when a prepared statement generates a result row.

method.name: trace_close
method.description: Event code for when a database connection closes.

method.name: progress_handler_disable
method.description: Disables the SQLite progress handler for the given database connection. Only passing null as the handler is supported in this wrapper due to C++ code generation constraints. This can be used to cancel any previously installed progress handler.

method.name: open
method.description: Opens an SQLite database file with the specified filename (UTF-8). On success, out_db receives the opened database handle. Returns SQLITE_OK or an error code.

method.name: open16
method.description: Opens a SQLite database using a UTF-16 encoded filename. The provided filename String will be converted to UTF-16 automatically. On success, sets out_db to the new SQLite database handle and returns SQLITE_OK. On failure, returns a SQLite error code and out_db is not modified.

method.name: open_v2
method.description: Opens a SQLite database using the specified flags and VFS module name. On success, sets out_db to a valid handle. Returns 0 (SQLITE_OK) on success or an error code on failure.

method.name: errmsg16
method.description: Returns the most recent error message for the given database as a String, converting from the UTF-16 encoded message returned by sqlite3_errmsg16.

method.name: error_offset
method.description: Returns the byte offset of the syntax error in the most recent failed SQL statement, or -1 if not available.

method.name: set_errmsg
method.description: Sets the error code and message for a database handle. If the error message is empty, the default message for the error code is used. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_NOMEM on OOM, or SQLITE_MISUSE if db is null or invalid.

method.name: limit_length
method.description: The maximum size of any string or BLOB or table row, in bytes.

method.name: limit_sql_length
method.description: The maximum length of an SQL statement, in bytes.

method.name: limit_column
method.description: The maximum number of columns in a table definition or result set of a SELECT, or in an index or ORDER BY/GROUP BY clause.

method.name: limit_expr_depth
method.description: The maximum depth of the parse tree on any expression.

method.name: limit_compound_select
method.description: The maximum number of terms in a compound SELECT statement.

method.name: limit_vdbe_op
method.description: The maximum number of instructions in a virtual machine program used to implement an SQL statement.

method.name: limit_function_arg
method.description: The maximum number of arguments on a function.

method.name: limit_attached
method.description: The maximum number of attached databases.

method.name: limit_like_pattern_length
method.description: The maximum length of the pattern argument to the LIKE or GLOB operators.

method.name: limit_variable_number
method.description: The maximum index number of any parameter in an SQL statement.

method.name: limit_trigger_depth
method.description: The maximum depth of recursion for triggers.

method.name: limit_worker_threads
method.description: The maximum number of auxiliary worker threads that a single prepared statement may start.

method.name: limit_parser_depth
method.description: The maximum depth of the LALR(1) parser stack used to analyze input SQL statements.

method.name: prepare_persistent
method.description: Hint to the query planner that the prepared statement will be retained for a long time and probably reused many times.

method.name: prepare_normalize
method.description: A no-op flag previously required for using the sqlite3_normalized_sql() interface.

method.name: prepare_no_vtab
method.description: Causes the SQL compiler to return an error if the statement uses any virtual tables.

method.name: prepare_dont_log
method.description: Prevents SQL compiler errors from being sent to the error log defined by SQLITE_CONFIG_LOG.

method.name: prepare_from_ddl
method.description: Enforces security constraints as if the SQL was derived from database schema DDL.

method.name: prepare
method.description: Compiles an SQL statement into a byte-code program for execution. On success, out_stmt is set to the prepared statement. The unused portion of the SQL string is not returned by this wrapper. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: prepare_v2
method.description: Prepares an SQL statement for execution. Returns a dictionary with 'error_code' (int), 'statement' (Ref<Sqlite3StmtHandle>), and 'tail' (String with unused SQL).

method.name: prepare_v3
method.description: Compiles a SQL statement into a prepared statement object using sqlite3_prepare_v3. On success, sets out_stmt to the created statement handle. Returns a SQLite error code. The unused portion of the SQL string is not returned.

method.name: prepare16
method.description: Compiles a UTF-16 encoded SQL statement into a prepared statement object. Returns the SQLite result code. The unused SQL tail is not accessible from this wrapper.

method.name: prepare16_v2
method.description: Prepares a UTF-16 SQL statement for execution. On success, sets out_stmt to a prepared statement handle. Returns a SQLite error code.

method.name: prepare16_v3
method.description: Compiles a SQL statement (UTF-16 encoded). Returns a Dictionary with the SQLite result code as 'result', the compiled statement as 'stmt', and the offset in bytes of the unused portion of the SQL string as 'tail_offset'.

method.name: sql
method.description: Returns the original SQL text used to create the prepared statement, or an empty string if unavailable. The returned string is managed by SQLite and is valid until the statement is finalized.

method.name: normalized_sql
method.description: Returns the normalized SQL text for the prepared statement, or an empty string if not available. Only available if built with SQLITE_ENABLE_NORMALIZE.

method.name: stmt_readonly
method.description: Returns true (non-zero) if the prepared statement makes no direct changes to the database file. See SQLite documentation for detailed caveats.

method.name: stmt_isexplain
method.description: Returns 1 if the prepared statement is an EXPLAIN statement, 2 if it is an EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN statement, or 0 if it is an ordinary statement or the handle is null.

method.name: stmt_explain
method.description: Changes the EXPLAIN mode for a prepared statement. If e_mode is 0, the statement behaves normally; if 1, as if prefixed with EXPLAIN; if 2, as if prefixed with EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN. May fail if the statement cannot be reprepared or is active.

method.name: stmt_busy
method.description: Returns true if the prepared statement has been stepped but not completed or reset. Returns false if the statement is null.

method.name: bind_double
method.description: Binds a double value to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

method.name: bind_int
method.description: Binds an integer value to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

method.name: bind_null
method.description: Binds a NULL value to a parameter in the prepared statement.

method.name: bind_text
method.description: Binds a UTF-8 string value to a parameter in the prepared statement. The 'destructor_flag' should be -1 (SQLITE_TRANSIENT) to ensure SQLite copies the string, or 0 (SQLITE_STATIC) if the string buffer is guaranteed to remain valid during statement execution. Using SQLITE_TRANSIENT is recommended and safest when passing Godot strings.

method.name: bind_text16
method.description: Binds a UTF-16 encoded string value to the prepared statement at the given parameter index. The value is copied by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT). Pass -1 for length to use null-terminated string.

method.name: bind_text64
method.description: Binds a string parameter to an SQL statement, specifying the number of bytes and encoding. You may control the destructor callback using standard SQLite macros or values.

method.name: bind_value
method.description: Binds a value to a parameter in a prepared statement.

method.name: bind_pointer
method.description: Binds an opaque pointer parameter to a prepared SQLite statement at the given index. The 'pointer' argument should be an integer representing a native pointer (void*). The 'type' describes the pointer type as a UTF-8 string. Custom destructor callbacks are not supported for safety; the pointer will not be automatically freed by SQLite.

method.name: bind_zeroblob64
method.description: Binds a zero-filled BLOB of the specified size to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

method.name: bind_parameter_count
method.description: Returns the number of SQL parameters in the given prepared statement. SQL parameters are tokens such as '?', '?NNN', ':AAA', '$AAA', or '@AAA' that act as placeholders for bound values.

method.name: bind_parameter_name
method.description: Returns the name of the N-th SQL parameter in the prepared statement, including any prefix (':', '$', '@', or '?'). Returns an empty string if the parameter is nameless or out of range.

method.name: bind_parameter_index
method.description: Returns the index of an SQL parameter with the given name for the prepared statement. Returns zero if no matching parameter is found. The name must be given in UTF-8.

method.name: clear_bindings
method.description: Resets all host parameter bindings on the prepared statement to NULL.

method.name: column_count
method.description: Returns the number of columns in the result set of the given prepared statement. Returns 0 if the statement returns no data (e.g., for an UPDATE).

method.name: column_name
method.description: Returns the name assigned to the specified column in the result set of a SELECT statement. Returns an empty string on error.

method.name: column_database_name
method.description: Returns the name of the database from which the Nth result column of the prepared statement was extracted, or an empty string if not applicable.

method.name: column_table_name16
method.description: Returns the name of the table for the specified result column of a prepared statement in UTF-16 encoding. Returns an empty string if not applicable.

method.name: column_origin_name
method.description: Returns the origin name of the column for the result set of a prepared statement, or an empty string if not available.

method.name: column_origin_name16
method.description: Returns the origin column name for the given column in the result set as a UTF-16 string, or an empty string if not available.

method.name: column_decltype
method.description: Returns the declared type of the Nth column of the result set of a SELECT statement, or an empty string if the column is an expression or subquery. The returned string is UTF-8 encoded.

method.name: step
method.description: Evaluates a prepared SQLite statement. Returns an SQLite result code (e.g. SQLITE_ROW, SQLITE_DONE, SQLITE_ERROR, etc.).

method.name: data_count
method.description: Returns the number of columns in the current row of the result set of the prepared statement. Returns 0 if the statement does not have results ready, is null, or has completed execution.

method.name: sqlite_integer
method.description: Code for the SQLite INTEGER datatype.

method.name: sqlite_float
method.description: Code for the SQLite FLOAT datatype.

method.name: sqlite_blob
method.description: Code for the SQLite BLOB datatype.

method.name: sqlite_null
method.description: Code for the SQLite NULL datatype.

method.name: column_text
method.description: Returns the value of the column as a UTF-8 string. Returns an empty string if the value is NULL.

method.name: column_text16
method.description: Returns the value of the specified column as a UTF-16 encoded string, or an empty string if the value is NULL.

method.name: column_value
method.description: Returns the value of the column in the current row of the result set for the given prepared statement.

method.name: column_bytes
method.description: Returns the number of bytes in the value of the specified column in the current row of the result set of a prepared statement.

method.name: column_bytes16
method.description: Returns the number of bytes in the value of the specified column as UTF-16 for the current row of the statement.

method.name: column_type
method.description: Returns the data type code for the value of the i_col-th column in the current row of the result set for the given prepared statement.

method.name: finalize
method.description: Destroys a prepared statement object and releases associated resources. If the statement was never evaluated or the last evaluation was successful, returns SQLITE_OK. If the last evaluation failed, returns the appropriate error code. Invoking on a NULL pointer is a harmless no-op. After finalization, the statement must not be used.

method.name: reset
method.description: Resets the prepared statement to its initial state, ready to be re-executed. Bound variable values are retained. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure. Bindings are not cleared.

method.name: create_function
method.description: Creates or redefines an SQL function or aggregate for the given SQLite database connection. The provided Callables are stored for use by user-supplied trampolines, but by default no trampolines are provided (all callbacks are null). This allows deletion of an existing SQL function or aggregate, but to register a new Godot-based implementation, the user must supply appropriate C trampoline functions elsewhere. The function name must not exceed 255 UTF-8 bytes. The app_data is stored in the context for callbacks. Returns the SQLite result code.

method.name: create_function16
method.description: Registers a new user-defined SQL function (scalar or aggregate) with the SQLite database using a UTF-16 function name. The Godot Callables are invoked as follows: scalar function: x_func(ctx_handle, [value_handle1, ...]); aggregate step: x_step(ctx_handle, [value_handle1, ...]); aggregate final: x_final(ctx_handle). All Callable arguments are Godot handle wrappers for the underlying SQLite pointers. The user is responsible for calling the appropriate sqlite3_result_* method on the context handle inside x_func or x_final. Returns the SQLite error code. The context struct's memory is not freed until the function is replaced or the database is closed.

method.name: create_function_v2
method.description: Registers a new SQL function or aggregate in the SQLite database. The Godot Callables x_func, x_step, and x_final correspond to the scalar function, aggregate step, and aggregate finalizer respectively. Each Callable is optional; pass an invalid Callable for unused callbacks. The Callables receive (context: Ref<Sqlite3ContextHandle>, args: Array of Ref<Sqlite3ValueHandle>).

method.name: create_window_function
method.description: Registers a new window function with SQLite. The provided Callables are used as callbacks: 'step' (sqlite3_context, argc, argv), 'final' (sqlite3_context), 'value' (sqlite3_context), 'inverse' (sqlite3_context, argc, argv), and 'destroy' (no args). Each callback receives Godot handle wrappers for the C context and value pointers. Returns an SQLite error code.

method.name: sqlite_utf8
method.description: Text is encoding as UTF-8.

method.name: sqlite_utf16le
method.description: Text is encoding as UTF-16 with each code point being expressed little endian (least significant byte first).

method.name: sqlite_utf16be
method.description: Text is encoding as UTF-16 with each code point being expressed big endian (most significant byte first).

method.name: sqlite_utf16
method.description: Text is encoding as UTF-16 with code points expressed in native byte order.

method.name: sqlite_any
method.description: Represents no preference for the text encoding; deprecated.

method.name: sqlite_utf16_aligned
method.description: Input strings are UTF-16 in native byte order and aligned to a 2-byte boundary.

method.name: sqlite_utf8_zt
method.description: Zero-terminated UTF-8 encoding for certain SQLite APIs.

method.name: sqlite_deterministic
method.description: The function always gives the same output with the same input parameters.

method.name: sqlite_directonly
method.description: The function may only be invoked from top-level SQL, not inside views, triggers, or schema structures.

method.name: sqlite_subtype
method.description: The function might call sqlite3_value_subtype() to inspect argument sub-types.

method.name: sqlite_innocuous
method.description: The function is unlikely to cause problems even if misused; no side effects.

method.name: sqlite_result_subtype
method.description: The function might call sqlite3_result_subtype() to associate a sub-type with its result.

method.name: sqlite_selforder1
method.description: Indicates the function is an aggregate that internally orders values of its first argument.

method.name: aggregate_count
method.description: DEPRECATED. Returns the aggregate count for the given context. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

method.name: expired
method.description: DEPRECATED. Checks if the given statement is expired. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

method.name: transfer_bindings
method.description: DEPRECATED. Transfers bindings from one statement to another. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

method.name: global_recover
method.description: DEPRECATED. Recovers from a global error state. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

method.name: thread_cleanup
method.description: DEPRECATED. Cleans up thread resources. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

method.name: memory_alarm
method.description: DEPRECATED. Sets a memory alarm with a callback. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

method.name: value_text
method.description: Returns the UTF-8 string representation of the sqlite3_value, or an empty string if the value is not text.

method.name: value_text16
method.description: Returns the value as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty string if value is NULL.

method.name: value_text16le
method.description: Returns a pointer to the UTF-16LE encoded text value for the given sqlite3_value. The pointer is valid only until the next SQLite call and should not be freed or modified by the caller. Returns 0 if the value is not text or cannot be converted.

method.name: value_text16be
method.description: Returns the value as a String decoded from UTF-16BE. If the value is not text or cannot be converted, returns an empty String.

method.name: value_bytes16
method.description: Returns the number of bytes in the value of this SQLite value object, interpreted as UTF-16 text (excluding null terminator).

method.name: value_type
method.description: Returns the datatype code for the supplied sqlite3_value handle (e.g., SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_TEXT, etc).

method.name: value_numeric_type
method.description: Returns the numeric type of the given SQLite value. The return value is one of the SQLite type codes, such as SQLITE_INTEGER or SQLITE_FLOAT.

method.name: value_nochange
method.description: Returns non-zero if the value has not changed during an update or insert operation.

method.name: value_frombind
method.description: Returns non-zero if the sqlite3_value object originated from a bind parameter.

method.name: value_encoding
method.description: Returns the internal text encoding of the sqlite3_value object. Result is one of SQLITE_UTF8, SQLITE_UTF16BE, or SQLITE_UTF16LE if the value is of type TEXT; otherwise, the result is meaningless.

method.name: value_subtype
method.description: Returns the subtype for an application-defined SQL function argument value. Use this to pass context between SQL functions.

method.name: value_dup
method.description: Creates a copy of the given sqlite3_value object. Returns a new handle, or null if the input is null, a pointer value, or on allocation failure.

method.name: aggregate_context
method.description: Allocates or retrieves the per-aggregate state buffer for a custom SQL aggregate function. Returns the pointer to the state buffer as an integer, or 0 if allocation fails or n_bytes <= 0.

method.name: user_data
method.description: Returns the user data pointer (as an integer) associated with the given SQLite user function context. This corresponds to the pUserData parameter passed when the function was registered. Must be called from the same thread in which the application-defined function is running.

method.name: context_db_handle
method.description: Returns a handle to the database connection associated with the given function context.

method.name: get_auxdata
method.description: Returns a pointer to the auxiliary data associated with the Nth argument value of the current SQL function context. Returns 0 if there is no auxiliary data.

method.name: get_clientdata
method.description: Returns the raw pointer associated with the given name for the database connection, as previously set by sqlite3_set_clientdata. Returns 0 if not set. This function is intended for advanced use, returns an unsafe pointer as an integer, and should not be exposed to scripts. Security warning: improper use can compromise process safety.

method.name: result_error
method.description: Sets an error result for a user-defined SQL function. The error message is passed as a UTF-8 string and the length in bytes.

method.name: result_error_toobig
method.description: Sets the result of a user-defined SQLite function to an error indicating that the result is too big.

method.name: result_error_code
method.description: Sets the result of the context to an error code.

method.name: result_int
method.description: Sets the result of an SQL function to the specified integer value.

method.name: result_int64
method.description: Sets the result of the current SQLite function context to the given 64-bit integer value.

method.name: result_null
method.description: Sets the result for the current SQLite function context to NULL.

method.name: result_text
method.description: Sets the result of an SQL function (within a UDF) to a UTF-8 text string. 'context' is the function context. 'text' is the string result. 'length' is the number of bytes to use from 'text' (or negative for null-terminated). 'destructor' is a pointer specifying how the library should manage the memory: pass SQLITE_TRANSIENT or SQLITE_STATIC, typically obtained via the corresponding wrapper functions.

method.name: result_text64
method.description: Sets the result of an SQL function to a string value, using the specified encoding and length. The destructor is always SQLITE_TRANSIENT (data is copied by SQLite).

method.name: result_text16
method.description: Sets the result of an SQL function to the given UTF-16 encoded text. The destructor argument should be SQLITE_TRANSIENT or SQLITE_STATIC. The length parameter specifies the number of bytes, or -1 for null-terminated input.

method.name: result_text16le
method.description: Sets the result of an SQL function to be the UTF-16LE encoded text provided in 'data'. The destructor parameter controls memory management: pass -1 for SQLITE_TRANSIENT (SQLite will make its own copy), 0 for SQLITE_STATIC (no copy, do not free), or a function pointer (as int64_t) for custom cleanup.

method.name: result_text16be
method.description: Sets the result of an SQLite user function to the given UTF-16BE encoded text. The 'text' argument must be a PackedByteArray containing UTF-16BE bytes. The 'length' argument specifies how many bytes to use from 'text'. The 'destructor' argument should be SQLITE_TRANSIENT or SQLITE_STATIC (as integer constants), or a pointer value for an appropriate custom destructor function, if needed.

method.name: result_pointer
method.description: Sets the result of a user-defined SQLite function to a pointer value, associated with a type string, and optionally a destructor callback.

method.name: result_zeroblob
method.description: Sets the result of the context to a BLOB of length n, filled with zero bytes.

method.name: result_zeroblob64
method.description: Sets the result of the SQL function to a zero-filled BLOB of the specified size in bytes.

method.name: result_subtype
method.description: Sets the subtype of the result for an application-defined SQL function using the given context. Only the lower 8 bits of the subtype are preserved in current versions of SQLite.

method.name: create_collation
method.description: Adds or modifies a collation associated with the database connection. The compare Callable should take arguments (user_data, PackedByteArray a, PackedByteArray b) and return int. Note: The user_data and Callable are kept alive for the lifetime of the collation; memory is not automatically freed by SQLite. Removing the collation leaks the context unless managed by the user.

method.name: create_collation16
method.description: Registers a new collation sequence with the database. The collation name must be a UTF-16 string. The x_compare callable receives three arguments: user_data (as passed), the first string, and the second string (both as Godot String). The callable must return an integer indicating the sort order.

method.name: collation_needed
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked when an undefined collation sequence is needed for the database connection. The callback receives the database handle, the desired text encoding (as int), and the collation name (as String).

method.name: activate_cerod
method.description: Specify the activation key for a CEROD database. Unless activated, none of the CEROD routines will work.

method.name: sleep
method.description: Suspends execution of the current thread for at least the specified number of milliseconds. Returns the number of milliseconds actually slept.

method.name: db_handle
method.description: Returns the database connection handle to which the prepared statement belongs.

method.name: db_name
method.description: Returns the schema name (as a String) for the N-th database on the connection. The returned string is managed by SQLite and may be invalidated after schema changes; copy if needed for long-term use.

method.name: db_filename
method.description: Returns the filename associated with the specified attached database name for the given SQLite connection. Returns an empty string if there is no such database or if the database is in-memory or temporary.

method.name: db_readonly
method.description: Returns 1 if the database named db_name on connection db is read-only, 0 if it is read/write, or -1 if db_name is not the name of a database on the connection.

method.name: txn_state
method.description: Returns the current transaction state of the specified schema in the given database connection. If z_schema is empty, returns the highest transaction state of any schema on the connection. Returns 0 (SQLITE_TXN_NONE), 1 (SQLITE_TXN_READ), 2 (SQLITE_TXN_WRITE), or -1 if the schema name is invalid.

method.name: txn_none
method.description: No transaction is currently pending.

method.name: txn_read
method.description: The database is currently in a read transaction. Content may have been read but not changed.

method.name: txn_write
method.description: The database is currently in a write transaction. Content has been written but not yet committed.

method.name: commit_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever a transaction is committed on the specified SQLite database. The callback must return an integer: return 0 to allow the commit or nonzero to convert the commit into a rollback. Passing a null Callable disables the hook. Returns the previous user data pointer as an integer.

method.name: autovacuum_pages
method.description: Registers or disables an autovacuum compaction callback for the database connection. The on_autovacuum Callable, if valid, will be invoked before each autovacuum operation. It receives (String schema_name, int n_db_page, int n_free_page, int n_byte_per_page) and must return the number of free pages to vacuum. If on_autovacuum is invalid, disables the callback. The on_userdata_destroy Callable is called when the callback is replaced or the database is closed, for user data cleanup.

method.name: update_hook
method.description: Registers or unregisters a callback to be invoked whenever a row is updated, inserted, or deleted in a rowid table of the database. The callback receives four arguments: operation (one of SQLITE_INSERT, SQLITE_DELETE, or SQLITE_UPDATE), database name, table name, and rowid. Passing an invalid Callable removes the update hook.

method.name: enable_shared_cache
method.description: Enables or disables the sharing of the database cache and schema data structures between connections to the same database for future connections. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. This function is omitted if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_OMIT_SHARED_CACHE. Use of shared cache is discouraged.

method.name: soft_heap_limit64
method.description: Sets or queries SQLite's soft heap memory limit in bytes. Pass a negative value to query the current limit without changing it. Returns the previous limit, or a negative value on error. Setting to zero disables the heap limiter.

method.name: table_column_metadata
method.description: Extracts metadata about a column of a table. Returns a Dictionary with the following keys: "data_type" (String), "coll_seq" (String), "not_null" (int), "primary_key" (int), "autoinc" (int), and "error" (int SQLite status code). Returns SQLITE_OK (0) on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: enable_load_extension
method.description: Enables or disables extension loading for the SQLite database connection. Extension loading is off by default. Enabling it may have security implications. Prefer using the db_config interface when possible.

method.name: auto_extension
method.description: Registers a Godot Callable as a static SQLite extension entry point to be invoked on every new database connection. The Callable will receive (Ref<Sqlite3Handle> db, pointer as integer to error message, pointer as integer to sqlite3_api_routines). Multiple Callables can be registered; all will be invoked for each new connection.

method.name: overload_function
method.description: Ensures a global version of the given function name and arity exists so it can be overloaded by a virtual table. Creates a placeholder function if necessary.

method.name: blob_reopen
method.description: Moves the blob handle to point to a different row within the same table. The new row is identified by rowid. If the row is invalid, the handle is aborted and cannot be used for further operations.

method.name: blob_close
method.description: Closes an open BLOB handle. The handle is set to null after closing. Returns the SQLite error code (0 for SQLITE_OK). Passing a null or invalid handle is a no-op.

method.name: blob_bytes
method.description: Returns the size in bytes of the BLOB accessible via the given open BLOB handle.

method.name: blob_read
method.description: Reads N bytes from the open BLOB handle starting at the given offset and returns them as a PackedByteArray. On error, returns an empty array.

method.name: blob_write
method.description: Writes data from the provided buffer into an open BLOB handle, starting at the specified offset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an SQLite error code on failure. The BLOB must be opened for writing. The buffer size determines the number of bytes written.

method.name: vfs_find
method.description: Finds and returns a SQLite virtual file system (VFS) object by its name. Names are case sensitive and must be provided as UTF-8 strings. If no match is found, or if the name is empty, the default VFS is returned. Returns an empty reference if no VFS is found.

method.name: mutex_held
method.description: Returns true if the specified mutex is held by the calling thread. Intended for use in assertions only. Returns 1 if the mutex pointer is null.

method.name: mutex_notheld
method.description: Returns true if the specified mutex is not held by the calling thread. Intended for use in assertions only. Returns 1 if the mutex pointer is null.

method.name: sqlite_mutex_fast
method.description: Mutex type: fast.

method.name: sqlite_mutex_recursive
method.description: Mutex type: recursive.

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_main
method.description: Mutex type: static main.

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_mem
method.description: Mutex type: static mem (used by sqlite3_malloc()).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_mem2
method.description: Mutex type: static mem2 (not used).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_open
method.description: Mutex type: static open (used by sqlite3BtreeOpen()).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_prng
method.description: Mutex type: static prng (used by sqlite3_randomness()).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_lru
method.description: Mutex type: static lru (lru page list).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_lru2
method.description: Mutex type: static lru2 (not used).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_pmem
method.description: Mutex type: static pmem (used by sqlite3PageMalloc()).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_app1
method.description: Mutex type: static app1 (for use by application).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_app2
method.description: Mutex type: static app2 (for use by application).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_app3
method.description: Mutex type: static app3 (for use by application).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_vfs1
method.description: Mutex type: static vfs1 (for use by built-in VFS).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_vfs2
method.description: Mutex type: static vfs2 (for use by extension VFS).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_vfs3
method.description: Mutex type: static vfs3 (for use by application VFS).

method.name: sqlite_mutex_static_master
method.description: Mutex type: static master (legacy compatibility).

method.name: file_control
method.description: Low-level control of database files. This method calls sqlite3_file_control(), allowing direct access to file control operations for the specified database. The db_name is the database name ("main", "temp", or as given in an ATTACH statement), or empty for the main database. The data parameter is passed as an integer value; only integer pointer opcodes are supported through this wrapper. The return value is the SQLite result code.

method.name: test_control
method.description: Testing and internal interface for SQLite. Allows reading and injecting state for SQLite testing purposes. The first parameter is an operation code; subsequent arguments vary by opcode. Only a limited subset of argument types are supported in this wrapper (int, String). Not intended for application use; interface is unstable and may change between releases.

method.name: str_finish
method.description: Destroys the sqlite3_str object and returns the constructed string as a Godot String. Returns an empty string if errors were encountered or if the buffer is null.

method.name: dbstatus_lookaside_used
method.description: Returns the number of lookaside memory slots currently checked out.

method.name: dbstatus_cache_used
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by all pager caches associated with the database connection.

method.name: dbstatus_schema_used
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the schema for all databases associated with the connection.

method.name: dbstatus_stmt_used
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap and lookaside memory used by all prepared statements associated with the database connection.

method.name: dbstatus_lookaside_hit
method.description: Returns the number of malloc attempts satisfied using lookaside memory (high-water value only; current value is always zero).

method.name: dbstatus_lookaside_miss_size
method.description: Returns the number of malloc attempts that failed due to requested size being larger than the lookaside slot size (high-water value only; current value is always zero).

method.name: dbstatus_lookaside_miss_full
method.description: Returns the number of malloc attempts that failed due to all lookaside memory being in use (high-water value only; current value is always zero).

method.name: dbstatus_cache_hit
method.description: Returns the number of pager cache hits that have occurred.

method.name: dbstatus_cache_miss
method.description: Returns the number of pager cache misses that have occurred.

method.name: dbstatus_cache_write
method.description: Returns the number of dirty cache entries that have been written to disk (excluding rollback/recovery writes). Overlaps with TEMPBUF_SPILL.

method.name: dbstatus_deferred_fks
method.description: Returns zero if and only if all foreign key constraints have been resolved. High-water mark is always 0.

method.name: dbstatus_cache_used_shared
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by pager caches, dividing shared cache memory evenly among connections.

method.name: dbstatus_cache_spill
method.description: Returns the number of dirty cache entries written to disk in the middle of a transaction due to cache overflow.

method.name: dbstatus_tempbuf_spill
method.description: Returns the number of bytes written to temporary files on disk that could have been kept in memory had sufficient memory been available. Overlaps with CACHE_WRITE.

method.name: dbstatus_max
method.description: Largest defined DBSTATUS verb value.

method.name: stmtstatus_fullscan_step
method.description: This is the number of times that SQLite has stepped forward in a table as part of a full table scan.

method.name: stmtstatus_sort
method.description: This is the number of sort operations that have occurred.

method.name: stmtstatus_autoindex
method.description: This is the number of rows inserted into transient indices that were created automatically in order to help joins run faster.

method.name: stmtstatus_vm_step
method.description: This is the number of virtual machine operations executed by the prepared statement if that number is less than or equal to 2147483647.

method.name: stmtstatus_reprepare
method.description: This is the number of times that the prepared statement has been automatically regenerated due to schema changes or changes to bound parameters that might affect the query plan.

method.name: stmtstatus_run
method.description: This is the number of times that the prepared statement has been run.

method.name: stmtstatus_filter_miss
method.description: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_MISS is the number of times that a join step was processed as normal because a Bloom filter returned a find.

method.name: stmtstatus_filter_hit
method.description: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_HIT is the number of times that a join step was bypassed because a Bloom filter returned not-found.

method.name: stmtstatus_memused
method.description: This is the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the prepared statement.

method.name: backup_pagecount
method.description: Returns the number of pages in the backup object referred to by the given handle. Returns -1 if the handle is invalid.

method.name: stricmp
method.description: Compares two UTF-8 strings in a case-insensitive manner, using SQLite's internal rules. Returns 0 if equal, <0 or >0 if not.

method.name: wal_autocheckpoint
method.description: Configures automatic checkpointing for the given database connection. If N > 0, a checkpoint is triggered after every transaction that results in N or more frames in the WAL file. If N <= 0, auto-checkpointing is disabled. This replaces any existing WAL hook callback, and is itself disabled if a custom WAL hook is set.

method.name: wal_checkpoint
method.description: Checkpoints a database by transferring the WAL content for the database named 'db_name' on connection 'db' into the database file and resetting the log. Equivalent to sqlite3_wal_checkpoint_v2(db, db_name, SQLITE_CHECKPOINT_PASSIVE, 0, 0). Retained for backwards compatibility.

method.name: wal_checkpoint_v2
method.description: Runs a checkpoint operation on the specified database or all WAL databases attached to the given database connection. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (SQLite result code), 'n_log' (number of frames in the WAL log), and 'n_ckpt' (number of frames checkpointed).

method.name: checkpoint_noop
method.description: Checkpoint mode: do no work at all.

method.name: checkpoint_passive
method.description: Checkpoint mode: do as much as possible without blocking.

method.name: checkpoint_full
method.description: Checkpoint mode: wait for writers, then checkpoint.

method.name: checkpoint_restart
method.description: Checkpoint mode: like FULL but wait for readers.

method.name: checkpoint_truncate
method.description: Checkpoint mode: like RESTART but also truncate WAL.

method.name: vtab_constraint_support
method.description: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to declare virtual table constraint support. Pass as the second argument; if set to 0, constraints are not supported (the default), otherwise the virtual table promises safe constraint handling.

method.name: vtab_innocuous
method.description: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to mark a virtual table as safe for use within triggers and views (innocuous).

method.name: vtab_directonly
method.description: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to prohibit a virtual table from being used from within triggers and views.

method.name: vtab_uses_all_schemas
method.description: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to instruct the query planner to start at least a read transaction on all schemas whenever the virtual table is used.

method.name: vtab_nochange
method.description: Determines if a virtual table column access is for an UPDATE where the column value will not change. Used within a virtual table's xColumn method for optimization.

method.name: vtab_collation
method.description: Returns the collation name for the given constraint in a virtual table's xBestIndex() context. If the constraint uses a COLLATE operator, its name is returned; otherwise, the column's COLLATE clause or 'BINARY' is returned. Only call this during xBestIndex.

method.name: vtab_rhs_value
method.description: Retrieves the right-hand operand value of a constraint in xBestIndex. Sets out_val to the value if available, or to null if not. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_NOTFOUND if the value is not available, or another error code.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_v2
method.description: Retrieves scan status information for a prepared SQLite3 statement. Returns a Dictionary with 'status' (int) and 'value' (Variant). The type of 'value' depends on scan_status_op: int for most values, double for 2 (EST), and String for 3 (NAME). See SQLite documentation for details.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_reset
method.description: Zero all sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus() related event counters for the given statement. Only available if built with SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS.

method.name: db_cacheflush
method.description: Flushes dirty pages in the pager cache of the specified database connection to disk for all schemas (main, temp, and attached databases). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_BUSY if locks cannot be obtained, or another SQLite error code on failure. Does not modify the database handle error state.

method.name: preupdate_hook
method.description: Registers a preupdate hook callback to be invoked prior to each INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operation. Pass an invalid Callable to unregister. Only available if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK.

method.name: preupdate_old
method.description: Writes into out_value a protected sqlite3_value containing the old value of the Nth column before update/delete. Only valid within preupdate callback for UPDATE or DELETE.

method.name: preupdate_count
method.description: Returns the number of columns in the row being inserted, updated, or deleted during a preupdate event.

method.name: preupdate_depth
method.description: Returns the trigger recursion depth at which the preupdate callback was invoked.

method.name: preupdate_new
method.description: Writes into out_value a protected sqlite3_value containing the new value of the Nth column after insert/update. Only valid within preupdate callback for INSERT or UPDATE.

method.name: preupdate_blobwrite
method.description: Returns the index of the column being written during a blob write in a preupdate event, or -1 otherwise.

method.name: system_errno
method.description: Returns the underlying operating system error code that caused the most recent I/O error or failure to open a file on the given database handle. The return value is OS-dependent and may correspond to system-level error numbers such as errno on Unix.

method.name: serialize
method.description: Serializes the specified database into a PackedByteArray. The 'schema' argument specifies which schema to serialize (e.g., 'main', 'temp'). The 'flags' argument can be 0 or SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY. If SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY is not set, the returned buffer is copied and freed after use; otherwise, only copied. Returns an empty array on error.

method.name: serialize_nocopy
method.description: Flags for sqlite3_serialize. SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY means that sqlite3_serialize() will return a pointer to the contiguous in-memory database that it is currently using, without making a copy of the database. If SQLite is not currently using a contiguous in-memory database, then this option causes sqlite3_serialize() to return a NULL pointer. SQLite will only be using a contiguous in-memory database if it has been initialized by a prior call to sqlite3_deserialize().

method.name: deserialize_freeonclose
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize: SQLite should take ownership of the memory and free it when finished (call sqlite3_free() on close).

method.name: deserialize_resizeable
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize: SQLite is allowed to grow the size of the database using sqlite3_realloc64().

method.name: deserialize_readonly
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize: The deserialized database should be treated as read-only.

method.name: carray_bind
method.description: Binds an array of data to the specified parameter of the prepared statement. The data is passed as a PackedByteArray (raw memory). This version always uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT for the destructor to ensure memory safety. Returns the SQLite status code.

method.name: sqlite_carray_int32
method.description: Data is 32-bit signed integers.

method.name: sqlite_carray_int64
method.description: Data is 64-bit signed integers.

method.name: sqlite_carray_double
method.description: Data is doubles.

method.name: sqlite_carray_text
method.description: Data is char*.

method.name: sqlite_carray_blob
method.description: Data is struct iovec.

method.name: carray_int32
method.description: Data is 32-bit signed integers.

method.name: carray_int64
method.description: Data is 64-bit signed integers.

method.name: carray_double
method.description: Data is doubles.

method.name: carray_text
method.description: Data is char*.

method.name: carray_blob
method.description: Data is struct iovec.

method.name: session_delete
method.description: Deletes a session object previously allocated using sqlite3session_create(). The session object becomes invalid after this call.

method.name: mutex_alloc
method.description: Allocates a new SQLite mutex or returns a static mutex pointer depending on the mutex type. Returns null if unable to allocate.

method.name: str_appendf
method.description: Appends formatted text to the sqlite3_str object using Godot-style formatting (`{0}`, `{1}`, ...). The format and arguments are specified as a Godot format string and an array of arguments. Internally, this formats the string in Godot and appends it using sqlite3_str_appendall. Note: SQLite's printf-style formatting is NOT supported through this wrapper.

method.name: str_errcode
method.description: Returns the error code for the given sqlite3_str object: SQLITE_OK for no error, SQLITE_NOMEM for out-of-memory, or SQLITE_TOOBIG if too large.

method.name: status
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information about the performance of SQLite. Returns a Dictionary containing the status code (as 'result'), the current value (as 'current'), and the highest recorded value (as 'highwater'). The meaning and validity of 'current' and 'highwater' depend on the specific status parameter passed as 'op'. If 'reset_flag' is true, the highwater mark may be reset after reading.

method.name: backup_init
method.description: Initializes an online backup operation from the source database to the destination database. Returns a backup handle on success, or null on failure.

method.name: snapshot_open
method.description: Starts a read transaction or upgrades an existing one on the given schema of the specified database connection so that it refers to the historical snapshot. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an appropriate error code. The database must not be in autocommit mode and both the schema and snapshot must be valid. This function is only available when compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT.

method.name: snapshot_cmp
method.description: Compares the ages of two valid snapshot handles. Returns a negative value if p1 is older than p2, zero if they refer to the same snapshot, and a positive value if p1 is newer. The comparison is undefined if the snapshots are not from the same database or were obtained before the last WAL file reset.

method.name: snapshot_recover
method.description: Scans the WAL file for the specified database and makes all valid snapshots available to sqlite3_snapshot_open(). Only available if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful, or an SQLite error code otherwise.

method.name: session_object_config
method.description: This method is used to configure a session object after it has been created. At present the only valid values for the second parameter are SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE and SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_ROWID.

method.name: session_objconfig_size
method.description: Option flag for enabling or disabling the sqlite3session_changeset_size() API.

method.name: session_objconfig_rowid
method.description: Option flag for enabling or disabling collection of data for tables with no explicit PRIMARY KEY.

method.name: session_indirect
method.description: Sets, clears, or queries the indirect change flag for a session object. Pass 0 to clear, greater than 0 to set, or less than 0 to query the current state. Returns 0 if the flag is clear, or 1 if set.

method.name: session_attach
method.description: Attaches a table to the session for change tracking. If the table name is empty, all tables in the database are tracked. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an SQLite error code on failure.

method.name: session_table_filter
method.description: Sets a table filter on a SQLite3 Session object. The filter Callable is invoked for each table not yet attached to the session to determine whether changes to the table should be tracked. The Callable receives the table name as a String and must return an int; returning 0 disables tracking for that table. Note: The filter Callable and context may remain in memory for the session's lifetime.

method.name: session_changeset
method.description: Generates a changeset from the session object. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int SQLite error code) and 'changeset' (PackedByteArray of the changeset data or empty on error).

method.name: changeset_size
method.description: Returns an upper limit (in bytes) for the size of the changeset that might be produced if changeset() is called on this session. Returns 0 unless the session was configured with SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE.

method.name: session_diff
method.description: Loads the difference between tables into a session. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'result' (SQLite error code) and 'error_message' (String with error details, if any).

method.name: session_patchset
method.description: Generates a patchset blob from a session object. Returns a Dictionary with the SQLite result code as 'code' and the patchset data as 'patchset' (PackedByteArray).

method.name: is_empty
method.description: Returns non-zero if no changes to attached tables have been recorded by the session object. Returns zero if one or more changes have been recorded. Note: Even if this returns zero, the changeset may still contain no changes if modifications were undone.

method.name: changeset_start
method.description: Creates an iterator used to traverse a changeset. On success, out_iter will be set to a new iterator handle and SQLITE_OK is returned. On error, out_iter is not modified and an SQLite error code is returned. The changeset_blob must remain valid until the iterator is destroyed.

method.name: changesetstart_invert
method.description: Invert the changeset while iterating through it. This is equivalent to inverting a changeset using sqlite3changeset_invert() before applying it. It is an error to specify this flag with a patchset.

method.name: changeset_op
method.description: Obtain information about the current operation from a changeset iterator. Returns a Dictionary containing the error code and, if successful, the table name, column count, operation type, and optionally whether the change is indirect.

method.name: pk
method.description: Obtains the primary key column definition for the current table in the changeset iterator. Returns a dictionary with the result code, a byte array where each entry is 1 if the corresponding column is part of the primary key, and the number of columns.

method.name: changeset_old
method.description: Obtains the original value of a column from a changeset iterator for DELETE or UPDATE changes. On success, sets out_value to a handle for the old column value and returns SQLITE_OK. Returns an error code and sets out_value to null if not successful.

method.name: changeset_new
method.description: Obtains the 'new' value for a given column from the changeset iterator. This is only valid for UPDATE or INSERT changes. On success, sets out_value to the new value or NULL.

method.name: changeset_conflict
method.description: Obtains the i_val'th value from the conflicting row in a changeset iterator during a conflict-handler callback. Sets out_value on success. Only valid in specific callback contexts. Returns the SQLite result code.

method.name: fk_conflicts
method.description: Returns a Dictionary with the integer fields 'result' (the function status code) and 'fk_violations' (the number of known foreign key violations in the destination database for the given changeset iterator). Should only be called from within an SQLITE_CHANGESET_FOREIGN_KEY conflict handler callback. On success, 'result' is SQLITE_OK; otherwise, SQLITE_MISUSE.

method.name: changeset_invert
method.description: Inverts a changeset buffer so that applying the inverted changeset reverses the effects of the original. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (the SQLite error code) and 'inverted' (the inverted changeset as a PackedByteArray, or empty if an error occurred).

method.name: changegroup_schema
method.description: Enforces that changesets added to the changegroup must match the schema of the specified database. Returns SQLITE_SCHEMA if the changeset is not compatible; otherwise returns an SQLite status code.

method.name: changegroup_add
method.description: Adds all changes within the changeset or patchset to the changegroup. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code on failure. The changeset data is provided as a PackedByteArray.

method.name: add_change
method.description: Adds the single change currently indicated by the iterator to the changegroup object. The rules for adding the change are as described for sqlite3changegroup_add(). If the change is successfully added to the changegroup, SQLITE_OK is returned. Otherwise, an SQLite error code is returned. The iterator must point to a valid entry when this function is called. If it does not, SQLITE_ERROR is returned and no change is added to the changegroup. Additionally, the iterator must not have been opened with the SQLITE_CHANGESETAPPLY_INVERT flag; in this case, SQLITE_ERROR is also returned.

method.name: changegroup_output
method.description: Obtain a buffer containing a changeset (or patchset) representing the current contents of the changegroup. Returns the binary buffer as a PackedByteArray on success, or an empty array on error. The buffer is managed internally and freed after copying.

method.name: changeset_apply
method.description: Apply a changeset or patchset to a database. All changes are enclosed in a savepoint transaction. The filter callback, if given, is called once for each table affected; returning zero skips changes for that table. The conflict callback is invoked for each conflict encountered during apply. Returns an SQLite result code.

method.name: changeset_apply_v2
method.description: Applies a changeset to the main database handle. Calls `x_filter(String table_name)` for each table, and `x_conflict(int64_t conflict_type, Ref<Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle> iter)` on conflict. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (the SQLite result code) and 'rebase' (PackedByteArray of rebase data, if any).

method.name: changeset_apply_v3
method.description: Applies a changeset to the database, invoking x_filter for each table and x_conflict for each conflict encountered. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (SQLite result code) and 'rebase' (PackedByteArray of rebase data, if produced).

method.name: changesetapply_nosavepoint_flag
method.description: Flag: Omit savepoint when applying a changeset.

method.name: changesetapply_invert_flag
method.description: Flag: Invert the changeset before applying.

method.name: changesetapply_ignorenoop_flag
method.description: Flag: Ignore no-op changes when applying a changeset.

method.name: changesetapply_fknoaction_flag
method.description: Flag: Foreign key constraints behave as NO ACTION.

method.name: changeset_data_type
method.description: Conflict handler type: Data conflict.

method.name: changeset_notfound_type
method.description: Conflict handler type: Row not found.

method.name: changeset_conflict_type
method.description: Conflict handler type: Primary key conflict.

method.name: changeset_constraint_type
method.description: Conflict handler type: Constraint violation.

method.name: changeset_foreign_key_type
method.description: Conflict handler type: Foreign key violation.

method.name: changeset_omit_result
method.description: Conflict handler result: Omit the change.

method.name: changeset_replace_result
method.description: Conflict handler result: Replace the conflicting row.

method.name: changeset_abort_result
method.description: Conflict handler result: Abort and roll back changes.

method.name: rebase
method.description: Rebases a changeset using the specified rebaser configuration. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (the SQLite result code) and 'buffer' (the rebased changeset as a PackedByteArray, or empty on error).

method.name: changeset_apply_strm
method.description: Applies a streaming changeset to a SQLite database. The input, filter, and conflict callbacks are provided as Godot Callables. The x_input Callable will be called with (input_ctx, PackedByteArray buffer) and must return a PackedByteArray containing the next chunk of data. The x_filter Callable will be called with (conflict_ctx, String table_name) and must return int; if not provided, all tables are included. The x_conflict Callable will be called with (conflict_ctx, int eConflict, Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle iter) and must return int. Returns the SQLite error code.

method.name: changeset_apply_v2_strm
method.description: Applies a changeset to the given SQLite database handle using streaming input. Callbacks are provided for input, table filtering, and conflict resolution. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int, the result code) and 'rebase' (PackedByteArray, the rebase data if generated, or empty if not).

method.name: changeset_apply_v3_strm
method.description:
Applies a changeset to the database using streaming callbacks.

Arguments:
- db: The SQLite database handle (must be valid).
- x_input: Callable for reading changeset data. Receives one argument (buffer_size:int). Return a Dictionary {buffer: PackedByteArray, result: int}, where buffer contains the bytes read and result is 0 for success or non-zero for error/EOF.
- x_filter: Callable for filtering tables. Receives one argument (Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle). Return int (non-zero to include, zero to exclude).
- x_conflict: Callable for conflict resolution. Receives two arguments: (int eConflict, Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle). Return int (resolution action).
- flags: Apply flags.

Returns: Dictionary with keys:
- 'result': The SQLite result code from the operation.
- 'rebase': (PackedByteArray) If rebase data was produced, contains the rebase as a byte array. Otherwise null.

method.name: changeset_invert_strm
method.description: Inverts a changeset stream using provided Godot Callables for input and output. The `x_input` Callable should accept an integer (max bytes to read) and return a PackedByteArray containing the data read (up to that size). The `x_output` Callable receives a PackedByteArray (data to write) and should return an int (result code). Returning a non-zero value from either callback may indicate an error and stop processing.

method.name: changeset_start_strm
method.description: Begins a changeset iterator using a streaming input callback. The x_input Callable is called with the maximum number of bytes to read and must return a PackedByteArray of data. The resulting iterator handle is set into out_iter. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code on failure.

method.name: changegroup_output_strm
method.description: Calls the provided Callable for each data chunk output from the changegroup. The Callable receives a PackedByteArray with the data and must return an int status. Returning non-zero from the callback will stop output and the function returns that value.

method.name: rebase_strm
method.description: Streams rebasing data using the provided input and output callbacks. The x_input callback is called with the requested buffer size (int), and must return a PackedByteArray with the data to read. The x_output callback is called with a PackedByteArray containing the data to write, and must return an int status. Returning nonzero from either callback signals an error.

method.name: session_config
method.description: Configures global parameters for the SQLite sessions module. This function is not threadsafe and must be called before any session-related objects are created. The 'chunk_size' argument is both input and output: pass the desired chunk size (in bytes), and on return it will be set to the final value used by the streaming interface. Returns a Dictionary with 'result' (the status code) and 'chunk_size' (the resulting chunk size).

method.name: tokenize_query
method.description: Flag for xTokenize(): Query mode.

method.name: tokenize_prefix
method.description: Flag for xTokenize(): Prefix mode.

method.name: tokenize_document
method.description: Flag for xTokenize(): Document mode.

method.name: tokenize_aux
method.description: Flag for xTokenize(): Auxiliary mode.

method.name: token_colocated
method.description: Flag for xToken callback: Same position as previous token.

method.name: create_module_v2
method.description: Registers a virtual table module with the SQLite database connection. The module name (z_name) and method table (module) are required. client_data is passed to xCreate/xConnect and will be provided to on_destroy (if supplied) when the module is destroyed. If on_destroy is provided, it will be called with client_data as its argument when the module is unregistered.

method.name: drop_modules
method.description: Removes all virtual table modules from the SQLite connection except for those named in keep_list. If keep_list is empty, all modules are removed.

method.name: declare_vtab
method.description: Declares the schema of a virtual table. Used by xCreate and xConnect methods of a virtual table module to specify the format (column names and types) of the virtual table via a CREATE TABLE SQL statement.

method.name: unlock_notify
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked when a required lock held by another SQLite connection is released. Only available if the library is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_UNLOCK_NOTIFY. See the SQLite documentation for full details.

method.name: vtab_distinct
method.description: Returns an integer (0-3) describing the DISTINCT/ordering requirements for a virtual table query, as determined by SQLite's query planner. This API should only be called from within a virtual table's xBestIndex method. The return value indicates how the output should be ordered or deduplicated: 0 = rows in orderBy order, 1 = group-adjacent rows, 2 = group-adjacent rows with optional duplicate omission, 3 = ordered with optional duplicate omission.

method.name: vtab_in
method.description: Identify and handle IN constraints in xBestIndex. Used by virtual table implementations during xBestIndex to query or specify whether a constraint is an IN operator and whether it should be processed all at once or individually.

method.name: vtab_in_first
method.description: Finds the first element on the right-hand side of an IN constraint for a virtual table xFilter parameter. On success, sets out_val to the first value and returns SQLITE_OK. If there are no more values, sets out_val to null and returns SQLITE_DONE. Returns an error code on failure.

method.name: sqlite_rollback
method.description: Conflict resolution mode: rollback.

method.name: sqlite_fail
method.description: Conflict resolution mode: fail the operation.

method.name: sqlite_replace
method.description: Conflict resolution mode: replace the existing row.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_nloop
method.description: Scanstatus option: total number of times that the X-th loop has run.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_nvisit
method.description: Scanstatus option: total number of rows examined by all iterations of the X-th loop.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_est
method.description: Scanstatus option: query planner estimate for average rows output per loop.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_name
method.description: Scanstatus option: name of the index or table used for the X-th loop.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_explain
method.description: Scanstatus option: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN description for the X-th loop.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_selectid
method.description: Scanstatus option: unique id for the X-th query plan element.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_parentid
method.description: Scanstatus option: id of the parent query element, or zero if none.

method.name: sqlite_scanstat_ncycle
method.description: Scanstatus option: number of cycles (processor time-stamp counter) for the query element.
